content.about_text: The University of Sydney, founded in 1850, is Australia's first university and a member of the Group of Eight research universities. Based in the city of Sydney, it offers one of Australia's broadest ranges of disciplines, with more than 400 areas of study.
content.why_yonsei: Sydney offers Yonsei students a fully English-taught semester or year with an exceptionally broad course choice — over 2,500 units across more than 400 areas — usually four units (24 credit points) per semester. Please note that postgraduate Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing, and Psychology, and clinical placements in any discipline, are not available to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The university's main campus, with its landmark sandstone quadrangle, sits just west of central Sydney, within easy reach of the harbour city's beaches, parks, and cultural life.

Students can take courses from these pillars - Design and Artificial Intelligence (DAI), Engineering Product Development (EPD), Engineering Systems and Design (ESD), Information Systems Technology and Design (ISTD), Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (HASS)
universities.restricted_areas: Architecture and Sustainable (ASD) subjects. Students may refer to https://bit.ly/SUTDsubjcatalogue on the list of subjects that they can apply for.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around July for spring.

content.about_text: The Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD) is a public university in Singapore focused on engineering, technology, design, and architecture. Its undergraduate education is organized into pillars and clusters that combine engineering and design with the humanities, arts, and social sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: SUTD is well suited to a Yonsei student in engineering, computer science, or the humanities who wants an English-taught, design-centered technical education in Singapore. Exchange students may take courses from the Design and Artificial Intelligence (DAI), Engineering Product Development (EPD), Engineering Systems and Design (ESD), and Information Systems Technology and Design (ISTD) pillars, as well as from the Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (HASS) offerings. Please note that Architecture and Sustainable Design (ASD) subjects are restricted; students should consult SUTD's subject catalogue for the list of subjects open to exchange applicants before applying.
content.campus_location: SUTD occupies a purpose-built modern campus in the Changi area of eastern Singapore, near Singapore Changi Airport, designed around interconnected studios and shared learning spaces.

video_links.label: University Exchange in Singapore with SUTD | Inbound Exchange

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around July for spring.

content.about_text: Thammasat University is a public university in Thailand, with a strong tradition in law, political science, and the social sciences. It operates several campuses, including the Tha Prachan campus in central Bangkok and the larger Rangsit campus in Pathum Thani just north of the city. The university offers a wide range of international programs taught in English alongside its Thai-taught programs.
content.why_yonsei: Thammasat offers numerous English-taught international programs that are open to exchange students, spanning business, engineering, law, international relations, journalism, economics, and area studies such as Southeast Asian and Thai Studies. This breadth makes it a practical choice for Yonsei students seeking coursework in English in Southeast Asia. Please note that medical fields are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: International programs are based primarily at the Tha Prachan campus in central Bangkok and at the Rangsit campus in Pathum Thani, north of Bangkok. Thammasat does not provide on-campus dormitories for exchange students but maintains a list of nearby private apartments.

useful_links.label: Faculty of Political Science: Exchange Students

video_links.label: [Official] Introduction - Thammasat University (Ver. Eng + Eng Sub | 5 minute)

universities.restricted_areas: Nursing and teaching papers

content.about_text: The University of Waikato is a research-led public university in New Zealand, with its main campus in Hamilton and an additional campus in Tauranga. It serves a diverse community of domestic and international students across a broad range of subjects. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei students can undertake a trimester exchange at Waikato, where a dedicated Exchange and Study Abroad Advisor supports incoming students before arrival and throughout the trimester. All majors are open to exchange students except Nursing and teaching papers, so course planning should account for this restriction. Campus housing is generally guaranteed for study abroad and exchange students who apply by the relevant deadline.
content.campus_location: The main campus in Hamilton sits close to the city center in the heart of New Zealand's North Island, a practical base for exploring the surrounding region. A second campus is located in Tauranga.

useful_links.label: How to Apply: Study Abroad and Exchange

video_links.label: Welcome to Waikato, New Zealand

content.about_text: The University of Otago, founded in 1869, is New Zealand's oldest university and is located in Dunedin on the South Island. It holds a strong academic reputation, particularly in fields such as health sciences, sport, dentistry, and anatomy, and the language of instruction is English. Otago has exchange agreements with more than 100 institutions worldwide.
content.why_yonsei: The University of Otago appeals to Yonsei students seeking an English-taught semester or year at a respected research university with a strong campus community. Exchange students must meet Otago's English language requirements and maintain full-time status, with a minimum of 54 points (three papers) and a maximum of 72 points (four papers) per semester. Please note that Medicine, Dentistry, Psychiatry, Physiotherapy, Pharmacology, Pharmacy, and other clinical courses are not open to exchange students, and programs such as the MBA, Wildlife Management, and the Master of Science Communication have restricted admission.
content.campus_location: The university is based in Dunedin, a coastal city on New Zealand's South Island known for its Victorian and Edwardian architecture, where students make up a large share of the population. Most students live in the community immediately around the campus.

useful_links.label: Academic Information for Visiting Exchange Students

content.about_text: The University of New South Wales (UNSW Sydney), founded in 1949, is a member of Australia's Group of Eight, known for engineering, business, science, and design, and based in the eastern suburbs of Sydney.
content.why_yonsei: UNSW offers Yonsei students a fully English-taught exchange in Sydney, where students take three or four courses per term across most faculties (the full list is in the UNSW Handbook). Please note that MBA courses, Honours-level (4xxx) courses, and courses at UNSW Canberra (ADFA) are not available to exchange students.
content.campus_location: UNSW's main campus is in Kensington, in Sydney's eastern suburbs, a short distance from the city centre and the beaches of Coogee and Bondi.

content.about_text: The University of Western Australia is a public research university located in Perth, the capital of Western Australia. It is a comprehensive institution offering a broad range of disciplines across the sciences, engineering, business, humanities, and the arts, and it is recognised as one of Australia's leading research universities. The primary language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: As an English-medium university, the University of Western Australia allows Yonsei exchange students to enrol directly in regular courses without an additional language barrier, with a standard full-time load of 24 points per semester. The institution offers particular strengths in the natural and environmental sciences, marine science, engineering, and business, and it also teaches Korean, Japanese, and other Asian studies. Please note that Medicine, Dentistry, and Graduate Psychology are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The main Crawley campus sits beside the Swan River in Perth, a short distance from the city centre. The campus is known for its landscaped grounds and heritage sandstone buildings.

video_links.label: Student Exchange and Study Abroad at UWA

content.about_text: The University of Adelaide is a public research university established in 1874 in Adelaide, the capital of South Australia, and is a member of the Group of Eight, a coalition of Australia's leading research universities. It is the third-oldest university in Australia and counts several Nobel Laureates among those associated with it, with English as the language of instruction. (Note: from January 2026 the University of Adelaide and the University of South Australia are combining to form Adelaide University.)
content.why_yonsei: As an English-medium Group of Eight university, Adelaide offers Yonsei exchange students access to a broad and well-regarded range of courses across one or two semesters. The input does not list field restrictions for this exchange, but students should review the university's course-selection guidance, as availability depends on prerequisites and meeting a full-time study load. Students are advised to confirm enrolment details with the current Adelaide University inbound study abroad team given the institutional merger.
content.campus_location: The North Terrace campus sits in central Adelaide, a coastal South Australian city, within walking distance of major cultural institutions. Semesters run from February to July and from July to November.

※ Australian universities may accept standard TOEFL iBT scores for admission purposes; however, English language requirements for an Australian Student Visa are separately determined by the Australian Department of Home Affairs (DHA).

As the final decision on visa eligibility follows DHA requirements, students without a TOEFL iBT Australia score may face difficulties in obtaining a Student Visa.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: Monash University is a large public research university based in Melbourne, Australia, and is a member of the Group of Eight, a coalition of leading Australian research universities. It teaches a comprehensive range of disciplines across faculties including arts, business and economics, engineering, information technology, law, medicine, and science. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: All courses are taught in English, and Monash offers a broad catalogue of subjects across most faculties, giving exchange students considerable flexibility in their study plan. Incoming students apply through the Monash Abroad inbound portal and should consult the Inbound Student Fact Sheet and Applicant Guide before applying. Please note that all practicums, clinical placements, and internships are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The main campus is at Clayton, in the south-eastern suburbs of Melbourne, Australia, with additional campuses across the Melbourne metropolitan area. The setting offers access to a major Australian city with extensive student services and public transport.

video_links.label: Why choose Monash University?
Study Abroad - Step out of the ordinary

content.about_text: Griffith University is a public research university in Queensland, Australia, with multiple campuses across the Brisbane and Gold Coast regions. It teaches across fields including business, the arts, sciences, health, and environmental studies, with English as the language of instruction. The university maintains more than 100 exchange partnerships worldwide.
content.why_yonsei: Griffith offers exchange students a broad selection of courses across its campuses and the chance to study in southeast Australia for one or two trimesters, earning credit toward the home degree. Please note that several fields are not open to exchange students, including Dentistry, Medicine, Nursing, Occupational Therapy, postgraduate Psychology, and the Master of Business Administration. Exchange students nominated by a partner university are not required to pay tuition fees to Griffith.
content.campus_location: Griffith operates several campuses across the Brisbane and Gold Coast areas of Queensland. The Gold Coast campus is located close to the region's well-known beaches.

video_links.label: Make it matter

content.about_text: Bond University, founded in 1989, is Australia's first private non-profit university, based on the Gold Coast in Queensland. It runs three semesters a year and is known for small classes and a strong international community.
content.why_yonsei: Bond offers Yonsei students a fully English-taught exchange on Australia's Gold Coast, choosing from a broad pool of subjects in small classes, with the beach nearby and a cohort drawn from more than 90 nationalities. Its three-semester calendar (January, May, and September) gives flexibility in when an exchange begins.
content.campus_location: Bond's campus is at Robina on the Gold Coast in Queensland, a sunny coastal region famous for its beaches, just over an hour south of Brisbane.

universities.toefl_subscores: Min of 22 in reading and writing
universities.available_areas: Undergraduate courses from all faculties
universities.admission_notes:
1) Applicants must have completed at least one academic year of undergraduate studies and must be a registered undergraduate in their home university throughout the study term
2) Students from non-English-speaking countries or from institutions whereby the language of instruction is not English, must fulfil at least one of the listed English language proficiency certifications.:

1)	TOEFL PBT: Min. of 583 (with min. 5.5 for writing & 50 for reading) OR
2)	TOEFL iBT: Min of 93 (with min. 22 for reading & 22 for writing) OR
3)	IELTS: Min. of 7.0 (with min. 7.0 for reading & 6.5 for writing) OR
4)	C1 Advanced: Min. of 185 (with min. of 185 for reading and 176 for writing)
universities.housing_info: SMU does not offer on-campus accommodation to exchange students. Exchange students are responsible to source their own housing. Upon successful enrollment at SMU, students will be provided with a list of alternative private housing options that extend corporate discounts exclusively to SMU students

content.about_text: Singapore Management University (SMU) is a public university established in 2000 and located in the downtown core of Singapore. Modeled in part after the Wharton School, it focuses on business, accountancy, economics, law, information systems, and the social sciences, taught through a seminar-style approach. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: SMU teaches entirely in English and, through its International Student Exchange Programme (ISEP), opens undergraduate courses across all of its schools to Yonsei exchange students. Applicants are generally expected to have completed at least one academic year of undergraduate study and to remain registered at their home university throughout the exchange. Its city-center location and interactive, seminar-based teaching style make it well suited to students interested in business, economics, law, and the social sciences.
content.campus_location: SMU occupies an integrated city campus in the downtown Bras Basah and Bugis district of Singapore, set among the city's museums, libraries, and arts venues.

useful_links.label: Centre for Global Education and Opportunities

video_links.label: Take A Tour of SMU's City Campus

content.about_text: The National University of Singapore (NUS) is a comprehensive public research university in Singapore, established in its current form in 1980 through the merger of the University of Singapore and Nanyang University, with origins tracing back to a medical school founded in 1905. It is organized into colleges, faculties, and schools spanning the humanities, sciences, design and engineering, computing, and the social sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: NUS is one of Asia's leading research universities and teaches in English, allowing Yonsei exchange students to choose courses from the College of Design and Engineering, the Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences, the Faculty of Science, and the highly competitive School of Computing. Please note that exchange in Business and Law is arranged only through department-level exchange agreements. Higher-level undergraduate modules (course codes 4000 and above) have very limited capacity and are often unavailable to exchange students, so applicants should plan their course choices accordingly.
content.campus_location: The main Kent Ridge campus is located in the southwestern part of Singapore, a green hillside setting connected to the rest of the island by an extensive public transport network.

video_links.label: NUS Because Video

universities.language_other_notes: Only accept international students without Singapore nationality.

content.about_text: Nanyang Technological University (NTU) is a public research university in Singapore offering programs across engineering, business, science, the humanities, arts, social sciences, medicine, and education. It hosts incoming semester exchange students through its GEM Trailblazer programme. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: All courses at NTU are taught in English, which makes it accessible to exchange students without a local-language requirement, and incoming students earn credit toward their home degree through the GEM Trailblazer semester exchange. Students must be nominated by their home university and meet the minimum GPA and English-proficiency requirements set by NTU. Course availability should be confirmed through NTU's GEM exchange programme pages when planning the study period.
content.campus_location: NTU's main campus is at Jurong West in the western part of Singapore, a large green campus often described as a self-contained university town. The university also maintains a presence in central Singapore.

video_links.label: A to Z of NTU

content.about_text: Yokohama National University (YNU), founded in 1949, is a national university in the port city of Yokohama, just south of Tokyo. Exchange students join its JOY Program (Joint Program with Overseas Universities).
content.why_yonsei: YNU offers Yonsei students an English-taught exchange through the JOY Program, with courses in the humanities, social sciences, and engineering taught in English specifically for exchange students; with adequate language skills and approval, students may also join regular faculty courses. English proficiency of around TOEFL iBT 80 / IELTS 6.0 (or Japanese JLPT N2) is required, and a Japanese-language course is offered.
content.campus_location: YNU's green Tokiwadai campus sits on a hill in Yokohama, Japan's second-largest city, a cosmopolitan port just 30 minutes by train from central Tokyo.

content.about_text: Waseda University is a private research university based in Tokyo, Japan, and one of the country's most prominent institutions across a wide range of disciplines. Its Center for International Education (CIE) coordinates university-wide exchange admissions. Courses are offered in both Japanese and English, depending on the school and program.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei students apply through the CIE after nomination by Yonsei, and only the schools and departments listed on the CIE exchange pages are open to exchange students. Each school sets its own requirements, including GPA and language proficiency, so applicants must confirm they meet all conditions for their intended school. University-wide open courses include offerings from the Center for Japanese Language, the Global Education Center, and the CIE, alongside selected courses from individual schools.
content.campus_location: Waseda's main campuses are located in central Tokyo, Japan's capital, within an urban setting well served by the city's transport network. The university operates several campuses across the Tokyo area.

video_links.label: Waseda is ready for you!

content.about_text: Keio University, established in 1858 by the reformer Yukichi Fukuzawa, is the oldest private comprehensive institution of higher education in Japan. Its historic Mita Campus, the symbolic heart of the university since 1871, is located in the Minato Ward of Tokyo, with additional campuses in Kanagawa and Saitama. Keio is one of Japan's most highly regarded private universities and played a notable role in the country's transition to a modern society.
content.why_yonsei: Exchange students from partner universities can join the Keio International Program (KIP), through which the International Center offers around sixty English-taught courses focused on Japan and East and Southeast Asia, alongside Japanese language courses. Some additional English-taught courses are available from individual faculties and graduate schools, though many faculty courses are taught in Japanese. Please note that physical education courses, foreign language courses, and courses in the School of Medicine are not open to exchange students, and Keio Business School (KBS) courses are likewise closed to general exchange students.
content.campus_location: Keio's main Mita Campus sits in central Tokyo's Minato Ward, near the National Diet and within easy reach of the city's business and cultural districts. The campus is known for its Meiji-era architecture and historic landmarks.

video_links.label: Keio Global | Shaping History, Shaping Tomorrow

content.about_text: Tokyo University of Foreign Studies (TUFS) is a national university in Tokyo, Japan, specialising in foreign languages, area studies, and international and intercultural studies. It offers a wide range of undergraduate courses, including Japanese language classes from elementary to advanced levels and English-taught courses focused on Japanese and intercultural studies. Each year the university accepts over 200 international students into its exchange programmes, who study for half a year or a full year.
content.why_yonsei: TUFS offers courses taught in both English and Japanese, so a Yonsei exchange student can study in English while developing Japanese language skills. Applicants must meet either an English requirement (CEFR B2) or a Japanese requirement (JLPT N2); courses taught in Japanese require demonstrated proficiency before registration, while many English-taught courses are open to all students. Please note that the Peace and Conflict Studies course at the Master level and Doctoral programs are not open to exchange students; please refer to the Course Guidebook for details.
content.campus_location: The university is located in the Tokyo metropolitan area, on the Fuchu campus in western Tokyo. The campus brings together students and faculty engaged in the study of languages and regions from around the world.

video_links.label: [Tokyo University of Foreign Studies］TUFS in One Phrase

universities.admission_notes: First- and second-year undergraduate students in four-year degree programs are not eligible for USTEP. Students enrolled in three-year undergraduate programs must have completed at least 1.5 years of their program before arriving at UTokyo.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around July for spring and around April for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Tokyo is a leading public research university in Tokyo, Japan, and one of the most prominent universities in Asia. It hosts incoming exchange students through its University-wide Student Exchange Program (USTEP), which offers a Type U track for undergraduates and a Type G track for graduate students. The university teaches in both Japanese and English.
content.why_yonsei: Through USTEP, more than 300 English-taught courses are offered each year across the humanities, social sciences, and STEM fields, alongside Japanese-taught options, so Yonsei students can build a study plan according to their English and Japanese proficiency. Applicants taking English-taught courses must meet a language requirement such as TOEFL iBT 90, IELTS 6.5, Cambridge C1 Advanced, or Duolingo 120. Students must be officially nominated by Yonsei and attend a mandatory orientation at the start of the term.
content.campus_location: The main campuses, including Hongo and Komaba, are located in central Tokyo, one of the world's largest cities, with extensive public transport. Orientation takes place at the end of September for the autumn term and the beginning of April for the spring term.

video_links.label: Introduction of USTEP (University-wide Student Exchange Program) at UTokyo

4. Major Studies Course: JLPT N2 equivalent or above is required basically. However, if the applicant can find an academic adviser who can give the guidance in a foreign language they wish to use, they can conduct their research in the foreign language (English, Thai, etc.). Please contact the Global Education Section in advance.
universities.available_areas: Students can apply for any faculties. However, the Faculty of Medicine seldom accepts international students because their field of study requires highly specialized knowledge, and most of the courses are already full of Japanese students.

2. The student must either be enrolled as a fulltime undergraduate student who has completed his/her first year of study at his/her home institution or be matriculated as a graduate student. Junior college students who apply must be in their final year of study.
3. The students must be proficient in Japanese at the level required by each given course.
4. The students must have a non-Japanese passport.
universities.housing_info: Please refer to pages 26-27 of the application guidelines: https://ges.skr.u-ryukyu.ac.jp/future-students/program/rise/

content.about_text: The University of the Ryukyus is the largest national university in Okinawa, Japan. For exchange students it runs the Ryukyus International Student Exchange (RISE) short-term program, built around Japanese-language study and the history and culture of Japan and Okinawa.
content.why_yonsei: The University of the Ryukyus gives Yonsei students the chance to study in Okinawa through its short-term exchange program (RISE), with tracks in Japan–Okinawa studies, glocal leadership, or a major-specific course. Most teaching is in Japanese, so Japanese-language ability is valuable; the program is designed to develop Japanese skills alongside subject study and exchange with local students.
content.campus_location: The university's main campus lies in the towns of Nishihara, Ginowan, and Nakagusuku on Okinawa's main island — a subtropical setting with a distinctive island culture and climate.

content.about_text: Sophia University is a private university in central Tokyo, Japan, recognised for its strongly international academic environment. It has been a pioneer of global education in Japan and has offered programmes in English for around sixty years, with more than three hundred partner institutions worldwide and roughly six hundred exchange students each year. The university offers degrees taught entirely in English in units such as the Faculty of Liberal Arts and the Faculty of Science and Technology English Course.
content.why_yonsei: Sophia offers more than three hundred and seventy English-taught courses, which makes it accessible to Yonsei students who do not have Japanese language ability, while Japanese-taught courses remain open to those who do. Its long-established English-medium programmes and large international student community make it a comfortable destination for an exchange in Japan. Exchange students should review the university's course information and registration restriction pages when planning their study, as availability depends on academic program and language ability.
content.campus_location: Sophia University's main campus is located in the heart of Tokyo, within walking distance of the National Diet, the Imperial Palace, and the State Guest House. The central location places students at the core of the city.

video_links.label: 四谷キャンパスドローン撮影／Drone Footage of Yotsuya Campus

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around October for spring.

content.about_text: Ritsumeikan University is a private university in Japan, and its Study in Kyoto Program (SKP) hosts exchange and short-term international students. The programme offers courses in Japanese language and culture as well as courses taught in English on Japan-related and business subjects, drawing on the historical and cultural resources of Kyoto.
content.why_yonsei: The SKP includes an Open Study in English Track for exchange students from partner universities, alongside Japanese-language tracks, so students may join with or without prior Japanese skills depending on the track. Please note that SKP students select courses only from the course lists distributed for each track and campus, rather than from the full range of regular courses, and that research guidance is not provided to SKP students. Yonsei students interested in Japanese studies, language, or a culturally immersive exchange in Kyoto will find a structured programme.
content.campus_location: The Study in Kyoto Program is based in Kyoto, a city that served as Japan's capital from 794 to 1868 and is home to numerous World Heritage temples and one of the highest concentrations of academic institutions in the country.

video_links.label: SKP (Study in Kansai Program) at Ritsumeikan University

universities.language_other_notes:

 2. English-taught Programs requires TOEFL iBT 79 or IELTS 6.0, TOEIC® L&R Test 780, PTE Academic 50, Cambridge English FCE Grade B

There are a handful of restricted courses, which can be seen on the following page: https://en.apu.ac.jp/abroad/prospective/incoming/course/List_of_Restricted_Courses_230626.pdf

content.about_text: Ritsumeikan Asia Pacific University (APU) is a private university located in Beppu, Oita Prefecture, in southwestern Japan. It is known for its highly international community, with students drawn from around ninety countries and regions and an enrollment split roughly evenly between domestic and international students. Courses are conducted in both English and Japanese, allowing exchange students to study in either language.
content.why_yonsei: APU is a strong option for a Yonsei student seeking an English-medium or bilingual experience in Japan, as undergraduate courses are offered in both English and Japanese, and exchange students may take undergraduate courses at the 100 to 400 level. Academic offerings center on Asia Pacific Studies (APS) and International Management (APM). Please note that the College of Sustainability and Tourism became available to exchange students only from Fall 2025, and a small number of individual courses are restricted; the official list of restricted courses should be consulted before finalizing a study plan.
content.campus_location: The campus is set on a hillside above the hot-spring city of Beppu in Oita Prefecture, on the island of Kyushu, with incoming exchange students housed in the AP House dormitory that supports integration into the local community.

video_links.label: Ritsumeikan Asia Pacific University Promotion Video “I’m APU” -Campus street- English ver

content.about_text: Rikkyo University is a private university in Tokyo, founded in 1874, with campuses in Ikebukuro in central Tokyo and Niiza in neighbouring Saitama. It offers exchange students a selection of English-taught courses alongside Japanese-language study.
content.why_yonsei: Rikkyo offers Yonsei students an exchange in central Tokyo with a range of English-taught courses — including Japanese studies, world cultures, and language courses — as well as Japanese-language classes. English-taught classes require a minimum level of English (around TOEIC 550 and above), and many courses are offered at both the Ikebukuro and Niiza campuses.
content.campus_location: The main Ikebukuro campus, with its ivy-clad red-brick halls, sits in one of Tokyo's busiest districts, while the Niiza campus lies in greener surroundings in nearby Saitama.

universities.language_other_notes: JLPT (Japanese Language Proficiency Test) N2 or above

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around September for spring and around March for fall.

content.about_text: Nihon University, founded in 1889, is one of Japan's largest private universities, with campuses across Tokyo and beyond. Exchange students typically join the Nihon University Japanese Language and Japan Studies Program (NU-JLSP).
content.why_yonsei: Nihon University offers Yonsei students an exchange in Japan centred on the Japanese Language and Japan Studies Program (NU-JLSP), combining Japanese-language study with courses on Japan. Please note that the Schools of Medicine, Dentistry (including Matsudo), Pharmacy, and Veterinary Medicine, and graduate-level study, are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: Nihon University is spread across many campuses, with its headquarters in central Tokyo; exchange students are based according to their programme, most often in or around the Tokyo area.

universities.language_other_notes: TOEFL iBT 79, IELTS 6.0, TOEIC 780, CEFR B2,  CET-6 485 or JLPT N1
universities.available_areas: All fields
universities.restricted_areas: None
universities.admission_notes: Nationality other than Japanese
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around March for fall.

content.about_text: Nagoya University, founded in 1939, is one of Japan's leading national research universities, based in the city of Nagoya, and counts several Nobel laureates among its alumni and faculty. Exchange students join the Nagoya University Program for Academic Exchange (NUPACE).
content.why_yonsei: Nagoya University offers Yonsei students an English-taught exchange through NUPACE, with more than 800 courses taught in English across all major disciplines, including the STEM fields. Students typically take 15 credits per semester and can combine courses in their major with Japan-area studies and Japanese-language classes; those with sufficient Japanese may also take regular Japanese-taught courses.
content.campus_location: Nagoya University's main Higashiyama campus sits in the eastern part of Nagoya, Japan's fourth-largest city and a major industrial hub roughly midway between Tokyo and Osaka.

universities.language_other_notes: JLPT N2 or above
universities.available_areas: Shinjuku Campus, Department of Japanese and Japanese Language Education

content.about_text: Mejiro University is a private university in Tokyo, with its International Exchange Center in Shinjuku. For international students its programmes centre on Japanese-language study, including a one-year intensive Japanese Language Program (JALP).
content.why_yonsei: Mejiro University suits Yonsei students who want to study Japanese language and culture in central Tokyo. The exchange is based at the Shinjuku campus around Japanese and Japanese-language education, with an intensive Japanese Language Program and an active international-student support community.
content.campus_location: The Shinjuku campus sits in Nakaochiai, Shinjuku, in the heart of Tokyo — a convenient base from which to explore one of the world's largest cities.

universities.language_other_notes:
1) ENGLISH: TOEFL iBT68, TOEFL ITP520, IELTS 6.0, University of Cambridge First Certificate in English with no less than Grade B, Duolingo English Test of 100 or TOEIC Listening and Reading of 820
 2) JAPANESE: Prior Japanese language study for a minimum of one year at the university level or JLPT N4 or a JPT score of 375 or higher.
universities.available_areas:
Students can choose courses from designated area.
 https://www.meijigakuin.ac.jp/en/international/students/academics_program/
universities.restricted_areas: Participants to this program take courses originally customized from Japanese Language, International Relations and wide variety of Japan-related studies. Participants who hold Level 1 of the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT) are allowed to take all UNDERGRADUATE courses offered at MGU upon prior permission from the instructor.

content.about_text: Meiji Gakuin University (MGU) is a private university in Japan founded in 1877, making it one of the oldest private institutions in the country, with a long history of international cooperation in education. Its International Student Program (ISP) provides a structured academic environment for the study of Japan and the Japanese language and welcomes exchange students from partner universities in Asia, Europe, North America, and Oceania.
content.why_yonsei: Meiji Gakuin is well suited to Yonsei students interested in Japanese language, international relations, and Japan-related studies, which form the core of the program offered to exchange participants. Course selection is drawn from a designated area of customized offerings; students who hold Level 1 (N1) of the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT) may take all undergraduate courses at MGU with prior permission from the instructor. Some Japanese-language ability is therefore important for making full use of the curriculum.
content.campus_location: Meiji Gakuin University is based in the Tokyo metropolitan area, with campuses in central Tokyo (Shirokane) and in Yokohama. The setting gives students access to one of the world's largest urban regions.

video_links.label: Meiji Gakuin University

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around August for spring and around May for fall.

content.about_text: Kyushu University is a national research university located in Fukuoka, Japan. Its main inbound exchange route for international students is the Japan in Today's World (JTW) program, which has run since 1994 and offers courses taught entirely in English. JTW accepts students for a single semester or a full year and does not require Japanese language proficiency.
content.why_yonsei: Through the JTW program, a Yonsei student can study at Kyushu University in English without prior Japanese ability, which makes it accessible while still offering immersion in Japan. Please note that practical courses such as Medicine, Pharmacy, and Dental Sciences are not open to exchange students, and courses offered by the Kyushu University Business School (QBS) are open only to exchange students enrolled in a Business School. A Yonsei student should confirm course availability against the university's exchange course list before applying.
content.campus_location: Kyushu University is based in Fukuoka, a major city on the island of Kyushu in southwestern Japan, with its main facilities on the Ito campus. Fukuoka offers a large-city environment in a region distinct from the Tokyo and Osaka areas.

universities.language_other_notes: For Japanese: Minimum JLPT score with grade N1 or equivalent is required.
universities.available_areas: All majors except for Medicine
universities.restricted_areas: Medicine

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around October for spring and around April for fall.

content.about_text: Kyoto University, founded by imperial ordinance in 1897, is the second oldest university in Japan and one of the former imperial universities. It is one of Asia's leading research institutions and has produced more Nobel Prize and Fields Medal-winning research than any other university in Asia, with nineteen Nobel laureates connected to it. The university is based in the city of Kyoto and is recognised internationally for its strengths across the sciences and humanities.
content.why_yonsei: Kyoto University offers two incoming exchange tracks: the Kyoto University International Education Program (KUINEP), which provides undergraduate-level lectures in English to mixed classes of international and domestic students, and the General Exchange Program, in which students take Japanese-taught faculty or graduate-school courses or conduct supervised research. The English-taught KUINEP track makes Kyoto accessible to Yonsei students across many majors, while Japanese-language ability widens the range of available courses. Please note that Medicine is not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: Kyoto University is located in Kyoto, Japan's former imperial capital, a city known for its historic temples, traditional culture, and large student population. The main campuses are spread across the city's Sakyo and surrounding wards.

video_links.label: YouTube

universities.language_other_notes:
1. Japanese-taught Programs requires JLPT N2.
 2. English-taught Programs requires TOEFL iBT 71 or higer.

content.about_text: Kwansei Gakuin University (KGU) is a private, nondenominational Christian university in the Kansai region of Japan. It hosts over 1,400 international students and maintains partnerships with more than 200 universities across some 40 countries. The university offers programs in Japanese and provides English-taught options for incoming exchange students.
content.why_yonsei: KGU's Contemporary Japan Program is designed for incoming exchange students and offers a Japanese Language Track and a Modern Japan Track, the latter focusing on learning about Japan through English-taught courses. Exchange students may also select additional English-taught electives, including undergraduate courses from the Schools of Sociology, Economics, Policy Studies, Human Welfare Studies, and International Studies. Please confirm specific course availability and any restrictions against the official exchange program page before applying.
content.campus_location: Kwansei Gakuin University is based in the Kansai region of western Japan, near the cities of Osaka and Kobe. Its main campus is known for its consistent Spanish Mission-style architecture.

video_links.label: 90秒で見る！関西学院大学 / A 90-second Kwansei Gakuin University overview! (2022)

universities.available_areas: Both International Relations (https://www.iuj.ac.jp/gsir/) and International Management (https://www.iuj.ac.jp/gsim/)

content.about_text: The International University of Japan (IUJ) is a private graduate university located in Minamiuonuma, Niigata Prefecture, Japan, established in 1982 as the country's first English-medium postgraduate institution. It comprises the Graduate School of International Relations and the Graduate School of International Management, and hosts a small, highly international student body drawn from many countries. All courses are taught in English.
content.why_yonsei: IUJ suits a Yonsei student seeking graduate-level study in English, as exchange students may take courses in both International Relations and International Management. Its English-medium, multinational environment allows study in Japan without Japanese-language prerequisites, and Japanese language classes are available for cultural immersion. The close-knit residential campus offers a distinctive small-community experience.
content.campus_location: The university is located in the rural region of Minamiuonuma, Niigata Prefecture, set in a quiet natural environment. The residential campus brings together students and faculty from many countries.

video_links.label: YouTube

content.about_text: Doshisha University, founded in 1875, is one of Japan's oldest and most respected private universities, based in the historic city of Kyoto. Exchange students join either its Global Education Module (GEM) or its Japanese Language and Culture Module (Nichibun).
content.why_yonsei: Doshisha offers Yonsei students an exchange in Kyoto through the Global Education Module (GEM), a set of interdisciplinary courses taught entirely in English on Japanese culture, society, and nature, studied alongside Japanese students (a B2 level of English is required, and students take up to three courses per term). Students can also take Japanese-language courses through the Nichibun module according to their level.
content.campus_location: Doshisha's historic Imadegawa campus sits in central Kyoto, next to the Imperial Palace, in a city famous for its temples, gardens, and traditional culture.

universities.language_other_notes:
*Law / Economics / Letters / Policy Studies / Global Management: JLPT N3 is suggested
 Chuo does not request an official language proficiency score, but students must be able to follow courses taught in either English or Japanese. Japanese certificates issued by home university are accepted. 
 *Commerce: JLPT N2 or equivalent, certificate is not necessary
 *Science & Engineering: JLPT N2 certificate is mandatory
 *Global Informatics: JLPT N2 certificate is mandatory
universities.available_areas: Law, Economics, Commerce, Science & Engineering, Letters, Policy Studies, Global management, Global Informatics (Global Informatics can be accepted only a few student)
universities.restricted_areas: Professional Graduate Program (Law School, Business School)

Fall Semester: March 30 (UG: 4.30)
Spring Semester: September 30 (UG: 10.31)
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around March for spring.

content.about_text: Chuo University is a private university based in Tokyo, Japan, with origins as a law school and a long-standing reputation in legal education. It offers faculties including Law, Economics, Commerce, Science and Engineering, Letters, Policy Studies, Global Management, and Global Informatics. Instruction is available in Japanese, with a selection of courses taught in English.
content.why_yonsei: Chuo University offers Yonsei exchange students study in fields such as law, economics, commerce, science and engineering, policy studies, and global management, with English-taught courses available that require no Japanese-language proof at application. Japanese language courses are offered at various levels, and the university covers part of the dormitory fees to provide accommodation at reduced cost. Please note that the Professional Graduate Programs (Law School and Business School) are not open to exchange students, and placement in Global Informatics is limited to a small number of students.
content.campus_location: Exchange students are based in Tokyo, with the International Center located on the Tama campus in Hachioji, in the western part of the Tokyo metropolitan area. The campus offers a green, spacious setting connected to central Tokyo by rail.

content.about_text: Aoyama Gakuin University is a private Christian university based in Tokyo, Japan. It offers programmes across the humanities, social sciences, business, economics, law, and the sciences, and it provides courses taught in both Japanese and English for international students. Instruction is available in Japanese and, for a selection of courses, in English.
content.why_yonsei: Aoyama Gakuin University offers Yonsei exchange students a selection of courses taught in English in fields such as economics, business, law, and international politics, alongside five levels of Japanese language courses, so students can study with or without prior Japanese. Applicants taking English-taught courses are generally expected to be native English speakers or to hold IELTS 5.5 or TOEFL iBT 68 or above, and undergraduate exchange students normally take at least 14 credits per semester. Please note that Physical Education classes, Teacher's Certificate courses, foreign language courses for Japanese students, and certain Department of Education courses are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The main Aoyama Campus is located in the Shibuya district of central Tokyo, while the Sagamihara Campus lies in neighbouring Kanagawa Prefecture, west of Tokyo. The Shibuya location places students in a central, well-connected part of the city.

video_links.label: AOYAMA GAKUIN UNIVERSITY_Become a Global Leader From AGU

content.about_text: Akita International University (AIU) is a public liberal arts university in Akita Prefecture in the Tohoku region of northern Japan, established in 2004. Modelled on American liberal arts colleges, it was the first university in Japan to teach all of its subject matter courses entirely in English. AIU is known for small class sizes, active learning, and a strongly international academic environment.
content.why_yonsei: Akita International University is well suited to Yonsei exchange students because all of its subject matter courses are taught in English, removing the Japanese-language barrier common at other Japanese universities. Short-term exchange students may take any subject matter course in the curriculum except those marked for degree-seeking students only. Please note that certain courses, such as English for Academic Purposes, capstone thesis courses, and study abroad preparation courses, are restricted to degree-seeking students, so students should consult the Course Offerings list when planning their study.
content.campus_location: Akita International University is located in the city of Akita in northern Japan, set on a wooded campus in the Tohoku region. The setting is quiet and residential, away from Japan's largest metropolitan centers.

video_links.label: A Quick Look at Akita International University

universities.language_other_notes:

universities.available_areas: Exchange students can register any faculty's undergraduate courses if they get permission from each professor on the first date of the class except for some special subjects such as PE, Language Class etc.
universities.restricted_areas: Architecture, GMBA (depending on individual case)
universities.admission_notes: No specific border line for GPA or English Scores/Test Types. However, students are required to submit designated documents for application.

content.about_text: Hosei University is a private university in Tokyo, Japan. Its Exchange Students from Overseas Program (ESOP), established in 1997, is a non-degree program for students who wish to experience Japan, drawing participants from over 200 partner institutions. The program's Japanese culture courses are taught in English, while Japanese language courses are also available.
content.why_yonsei: Hosei's ESOP allows exchange students to take English-taught courses on traditional and modern Japan, alongside Japanese Language Education courses at no additional fee. With permission from the instructor, exchange students may also register for undergraduate courses across faculties, except for certain subjects such as physical education and language classes. Please note that Architecture and the GMBA program may be restricted depending on the individual case, and Hosei provides a rent subsidy for designated dormitories as part of its scholarship support.
content.campus_location: Hosei University's main Ichigaya campus is located in central Tokyo, the capital and largest city of Japan. The central setting gives students convenient access to the city.

video_links.label: Hosei University（English）

universities.language_other_notes:
TOEFL iBT 80 or IELTS 6.5
(If applicants are not able to provide one of these English certificates, they will need to submit a certificate issued by their home universities to certify that their English proficiency meets CEFR B2.)

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around August for spring.

content.about_text: National Taiwan Normal University (NTNU) is a public university in Taipei, Taiwan, founded in 1922. It offers study across a wide range of fields and is particularly noted for education, linguistics, the performing arts, and modern languages. The university provides a large number of English-taught courses and has been recognized by the Ministry of Education as a Bilingual Benchmark University.
content.why_yonsei: NTNU offers a wide selection of English-taught courses, so there is no prior Chinese language requirement, and students may also take Mandarin Chinese language classes. Through the NTU System partnership, enrolled students gain access to the resources of NTNU, National Taiwan University, and National Taiwan University of Science and Technology, including cross-registration and shared facilities. Please note that courses from the School of Teacher Education and personal instrument instruction in the College of Music are not open to exchange students, and certain interdisciplinary and global-studies programmes are not open to native Chinese speakers.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in the Da'an District in central Taipei, placing students within easy reach of the city's transport, cultural sites, and amenities.

video_links.label: Come Study at NTNU! (National Taiwan Normal University)

content.about_text: National Tsing Hua University (NTHU) is a national public research university in Hsinchu, Taiwan, re-established in Taiwan in 1956 and developed into a comprehensive research university spanning science, technology, engineering, humanities, social sciences, and management. It enrolls roughly 9,000 undergraduate and 9,000 graduate students. Mandarin is the primary language of instruction, with a selection of courses and some degree programs offered in English.
content.why_yonsei: NTHU is a strong choice for Yonsei students in science, technology, and engineering, given its location at the heart of Taiwan's semiconductor and research cluster, though students will also find offerings in the humanities, social sciences, and management. A selection of English-taught courses is available to exchange students, so students should review the current course list for their intended fields. Incoming students should note that university housing is available but not guaranteed.
content.campus_location: NTHU is located in Hsinchu, in northern Taiwan, adjacent to the Hsinchu Science Park and major national research institutes. The location places the campus at the center of Taiwan's high-technology industry.

video_links.label: National Tsing Hua University -- Your Best Choice

universities.language_other_notes:
1. Students should meet either English or Chinese language proficiency at: English language proficiency of at least CEFR B2. For example, TOEFL iBT: 71, or IELTS: 5.5 or Chinese language proficiency of at least CEFR B1. For example, Test of Chinese as a Foreign Language (TOCFL): Band B Level 3, or Chinese Proficiency Test (HSK): Level 5 
 2. Students should hold a non-Taiwanese passport. Students of Taiwanese nationality alone are not eligible for the exchange program. Only students with dual citizenship, where one is of foreign origin, are eligible for the exchange program.

universities.restricted_areas: https://oia.ntu.edu.tw/en/internationalstudents/exchange/overview
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in September for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: National Taiwan University (NTU) is a leading comprehensive public research university in Taipei, Taiwan, offering programmes across the humanities, sciences, engineering, medicine, social sciences, law, and management. Courses are taught mainly in English or Chinese, and the university hosts a large international exchange community.
content.why_yonsei: Incoming exchange students may take subjects in English, study Mandarin Chinese for credit, or, with sufficient proficiency, enroll in regular Chinese-taught classes. NTU requires exchange students to take at least two courses or four NTU credits per semester, while also meeting their home university requirements. Please note that some departments, such as Anthropology, require high-level Chinese proficiency because their courses are not offered in English, so course planning should account for the language of instruction in each field.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in the Da'an District of Taipei, a large green campus close to the city centre and well served by public transport.

video_links.label: Where Great Minds Gather | National Taiwan University

universities.available_areas: https://www.nsysu.edu.tw/p/412-1000-88.php?Lang=en
universities.restricted_areas: International Business Bachelor Program, Master of Business Administration Program in International Business, Global Human Resource Management MBA Program, Si-Wan College.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around August for spring and around March for fall.

content.about_text: National Sun Yat-sen University (NSYSU) is a public research university located in the Sizihwan district of Kaohsiung, Taiwan. Each year more than 1,200 international students come to NSYSU for degree programs, exchange, research, or language study. The languages of instruction are Chinese and English, with a more limited range of courses available in English.
content.why_yonsei: NSYSU offers Yonsei students a research university setting with a scenic coastal campus and a portfolio of English-taught courses, though students should confirm the English course list for their intended fields each semester. Please note that several units are not open to exchange students, including the International Business Bachelor Program, the MBA Program in International Business, the Global Human Resource Management MBA Program, and Si-Wan College. The university operates on a Fall (September to January) and Spring (February to June) calendar.
content.campus_location: The campus is set in the Sizihwan area of Kaohsiung, on Taiwan's southwestern coast, with views of the ocean and surrounding mountains. Kaohsiung is Taiwan's major southern port city.

video_links.label: NSYSU | Be the Best You Can Be
Introduction of National Sun Yat-sen University: A highly research intensive university in Taiwan

universities.language_other_notes:
1. Mandarin-taught courses requires a minimum of TOCFL Superior or HSK Level 4
 2. English-taught courses requires TOEFL iBT 80, IELTS 6.0, TOEIC 700, or CEFR B2
universities.available_areas: Commerce, Communication, Education, Foreign Languages & Literature, International Affairs, Law, Liberal Arts, Science, Social Sciences, International College of Innovation, Informatics
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: National Chengchi University (NCCU) is a public university in Taipei, Taiwan, well known for its strengths in the humanities, social sciences, commerce, communication, law, and international affairs. The university offers a large number of English-taught courses across many departments each year.
content.why_yonsei: NCCU offers a substantial selection of English-taught courses, and no prior Chinese language skills are required to participate, though learning some Mandarin is encouraged. Exchange students may take courses across departments, but please note that certain programmes are not open to exchange students, including the International Master's Program in Asia-Pacific Studies, the International and Executive MBA programmes, the College of Global Banking and Finance, and on-job training programmes; students also cannot take courses offered by the department of their own native language. Its strengths in commerce, communication, and international affairs make it well suited to students in those fields.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in the Wenshan District in the southern part of Taipei, set against a hillside and surrounded by greenery while remaining connected to the city centre by public transport.

video_links.label: [English Subtitle] A Brief Introduction of NCCU

universities.language_other_notes: Please check department requirements: https://oia.ncku.edu.tw/var/file/32/1032/img/4576/113DepartmentrequirementsAY2024-25-ENG.pdf

content.about_text: National Cheng Kung University (NCKU), founded in 1931, is one of Taiwan's leading research universities, based in the southern city of Tainan. It is especially well known for engineering, the sciences, planning and design, and medicine.
content.why_yonsei: NCKU offers Yonsei students an exchange in Tainan, Taiwan's historic former capital, with English-taught courses offered every semester — though the proportion taught in English varies by department. Students are usually expected to take at least one course from their host department, and proof of English proficiency at around B1 to B2 is required.
content.campus_location: NCKU is spread across several campuses in the heart of Tainan, a warm, food-loving city in southern Taiwan known for its temples and history.

universities.language_other_notes: Student has undertaken a minimum of one-year university level study by the time they begin their exchange studies at HKU. Both native and non-native English Speakers must provide valid and acceptable English Proficiency Proof: https://intlaffairs.hku.hk/backend/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/HKU-English-Proficiency-Proof-Requirement-1.pdf
universities.available_areas: Architecture, Arts, Business and Economics, Education, Engineering, Law, Science, Social Sciences
universities.restricted_areas: Faculty of Dentistry, Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine, BSc (Exercise and Health) Programme, Most final year project courses provided by all faculties
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around February for spring.

content.about_text: The University of Hong Kong (HKU) is a public research university founded in 1911, making it the oldest tertiary institution in Hong Kong. It is organized into faculties including Architecture, Arts, Business and Economics, Education, Engineering, Law, Science, and Social Sciences, and is consistently ranked among Asia's leading universities. The language of instruction is predominantly English.
content.why_yonsei: With English as the main medium of instruction, Yonsei exchange students can enroll across a wide range of faculties such as Architecture, Arts, Business and Economics, Education, Engineering, Law, Science, and Social Sciences. Please note that the Faculty of Dentistry, the Li Ka Shing Faculty of Medicine, and the BSc (Exercise and Health) Programme are not open to exchange students, and most final-year project courses across all faculties are also unavailable.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in Pokfulam on the western side of Hong Kong Island, set on hillsides above the city and built in a mix of historic and modern architectural styles. Its location gives students direct access to the urban core of Hong Kong.

video_links.label: I am an HKUer, at the best I am!

universities.language_other_notes: Student should possess English proficiency equivalent to IELTS 6.0 or TOEFL 80 (internet-based). Incoming Exchange students are not required to submit proof of English proficiency at the time of application. However, they may be asked to provide such a document when needed.
universities.available_areas: https://www.polyu.edu.hk/geo/exchange-and-study-abroad/incoming-students/incoming-exchange/
universities.restricted_areas: At least 50% of students' enrolled subjects must come from the Host department. The rest of study load can be subjects offered by other departments. All registrations are subject to the approval of the respective departments.

content.about_text: The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU) is a public research university in Hong Kong, recognized for its programs in engineering, design, business, hotel and tourism management, and applied sciences. English is the medium of instruction across its subjects. The university hosts exchange students through its Global Engagement Office.
content.why_yonsei: As an English-medium university, PolyU is accessible to Yonsei exchange students who meet the host language requirements, and it offers strengths in design, engineering, business, and hospitality. Exchange students are expected to complete a minimum of 9 and a maximum of 18 credits per semester, with most subjects carrying 3 credits. Please note that at least 50 percent of a student's enrolled subjects must be taken in the host department, with the remainder open to other departments subject to departmental approval.
content.campus_location: PolyU is located in Hung Hom, Kowloon, in the heart of Hong Kong, with strong public transport links across the city. Exchange students may be eligible for accommodation at the PolyU Student Halls of Residence depending on the exchange agreement.

video_links.label: YouTube

content.about_text: The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK), founded in 1963, is a comprehensive research university known for its collegiate system and its large, green hillside campus in Sha Tin. It teaches across the Arts, Business, Engineering, Law, Medicine, Science, Social Science, and Education, with English, Cantonese, and Mandarin used as languages of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: CUHK offers Yonsei students a wide choice of English-taught courses across many faculties on one of Asia's most striking campuses, and Chinese-language ability is not required. A minimum GPA of around 3.0/4.0 is recommended. Note that some Law and Bachelor of Arts courses have access restrictions for exchange students, so it is worth checking the course-exception list when planning a study plan.
content.campus_location: CUHK occupies a large, hillside campus in Sha Tin in Hong Kong's New Territories, organised into residential colleges and linked to the city by its own MTR station, with views over Tolo Harbour.

video_links.label: CUHK Student Exchange 2023

content.about_text: City University of Hong Kong is a public research university located in Hong Kong. It offers programmes across business, science and engineering, liberal arts and social sciences, data science, creative media, and other fields, and English is the principal language of instruction. The university is organised around its Global Engagement Office for international exchange.
content.why_yonsei: City University of Hong Kong is an English-medium university, which allows Yonsei exchange students to enrol directly across a wide range of disciplines. Exchange students take a minimum of 12 credit units in a regular semester to meet immigration requirements, and the Global Engagement Office runs mandatory orientation and induction programmes before the semester begins. Please note that Veterinary Medicine is not open to exchange students, and some individual courses are unavailable or require equivalent prerequisites.
content.campus_location: The university's main campus is located in the Kowloon Tong area of Kowloon, in central Hong Kong. It is highly accessible by the city's metro and rail network.

video_links.label: CityU Exchange Programme

universities.language_other_notes:
1.For Chinese-taught Undergraduate/Graduate Courses

Applicants must have HSK certificate, generally with a score over 180 in HSK 5. 

2.For English-taught Undergraduate/Graduate Courses

Applicants from non-English-speaking backgrounds are required to provide an IELTS score of 6.0, a TOEFL iBT score of 88, or a CEFR B2 level certification. Alternatively, an official English proficiency certificate issued by the applicant’s home university (e.g., a language proficiency letter or medium-of-instruction statement) is also acceptable. IELTS retakes and TOEFL MyBest score are accepted.
universities.available_areas: Most of courses are open to exchange student
universities.restricted_areas: ZJE,MBBS
universities.admission_notes: Students with Chinese citizenship are not eligible to apply

content.about_text: Zhejiang University (ZJU), founded in 1897, is one of China's top universities and a member of the elite C9 League, based in the scenic city of Hangzhou. It offers exchange students a range of English-taught and bilingual courses.
content.why_yonsei: ZJU offers Yonsei students an exchange at one of China's leading universities in Hangzhou, with most courses open to exchange students and a selection taught in English or bilingually (for example in economics and international studies); Chinese-taught courses are also available. Please note that the ZJE (Zhejiang University–Edinburgh Institute) and the MBBS medical programme are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: ZJU's main Zijingang campus sits in Hangzhou, a historic city famed for the scenery of West Lake and now a major centre of China's technology industry, about an hour from Shanghai by high-speed rail.

universities.restricted_areas: https://iso.zjnu.edu.cn/2018ngjxszszyml/list.htm
universities.admission_notes: https://iso.zjnu.edu.cn/ywb/main.htm

content.about_text: Zhejiang Normal University (ZJNU), founded in 1956, is a comprehensive university based in Jinhua in Zhejiang Province, China, with a strong tradition in teacher education and a student body drawn from over 100 countries.
content.why_yonsei: ZJNU offers Yonsei students an exchange in Jinhua, in eastern China, with a selection of courses taught in English alongside Chinese-taught programmes and Chinese-language study (Chinese-taught study requires an HSK certificate, while English-taught courses require IELTS or TOEFL). The university has a particular strength in education.
content.campus_location: ZJNU's main campus is in Jinhua, a mid-sized city in Zhejiang Province in eastern China, within easy reach of Hangzhou and Shanghai by high-speed rail.

universities.restricted_areas: https://intl-nondegree.tsinghua.edu.cn/f/login

content.about_text: Tsinghua University is a leading public research university located in Beijing, China. It is widely regarded as one of China's most prestigious institutions and has established university-level student exchange agreements with more than 120 universities worldwide. The main language of instruction is Chinese, though the university also offers over 200 courses taught in English.
content.why_yonsei: A Yonsei exchange student at Tsinghua can choose from its English-taught courses as well as Chinese language learning options. Applicants from non-native English-speaking countries taking English-taught courses are generally required to submit proof of English proficiency at or above TOEFL 90 or IELTS 6.5, and students should confirm the language of instruction with the relevant academic department. Course availability and any restrictions are managed through the university's Non-Degree International Student Portal.
content.campus_location: Tsinghua University's campus is in northwestern Beijing, in the Haidian district near several other major universities. The historic, garden-like campus is known for its scale and academic atmosphere.

video_links.label: Welcome to Tsinghua!

universities.university_name: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
universities.available_areas: Business & Management and other non-business areas, including Science, Humanities & Social Sciences, Engineering
universities.restricted_areas: Postgraduate courses
universities.admission_notes: Students must be majoring in business and have completed at least two year of study at the home institution at the time of application.

content.about_text: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST) is a public research university in Hong Kong, organized around the Schools of Science, Engineering, Business and Management, and Humanities and Social Science. It is internationally recognized for its strengths in science, technology, engineering, and business. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: All teaching is in English, and exchange opportunities span Business and Management as well as non-business areas including Science, Engineering, and Humanities and Social Sciences, so Yonsei students have broad course choice. Please note that postgraduate courses are not open to exchange students. Applicants are normally expected to have completed about two years of undergraduate study and to meet an English proficiency level equivalent to TOEFL iBT 80 or IELTS 6.0.
content.campus_location: HKUST occupies a large seafront campus overlooking Clear Water Bay on the Sai Kung Peninsula in the eastern New Territories of Hong Kong. The area is known for its natural scenery and outdoor recreation, with university-arranged housing available on or near campus.

video_links.label: HKUST Aerial - Campus Drone Tour
EMPOWERING UNLIMITED POSSIBILITIES

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around July for spring.

content.about_text: The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen (CUHK-Shenzhen) is a comprehensive research university established in 2014 under the educational philosophy and academic system of The Chinese University of Hong Kong. Located in Shenzhen in Guangdong Province, China, it follows a college system and general-education model and emphasizes bilingual teaching in English and Chinese, with English used as the main medium of instruction across its programs.
content.why_yonsei: CUHK-Shenzhen offers extensive English-taught curricula, and exchange students may choose courses across all schools where they meet the prerequisites and space allows, while interested students can also take Chinese-language courses at a suitable level. Please note that Clinical Medicine and all majors in the School of Music are not open to exchange students. The city of Shenzhen is a major technology hub, home to companies such as Huawei, Tencent, and BYD, which can be of interest to students in business, engineering, and related fields.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in the Longgang District of Shenzhen, a young and fast-growing city in southern China bordering Hong Kong. The surrounding region is known for its technology industry and extensive green space.

useful_links.label: Incoming Students - Office of Academic Links

video_links.label: Introduction to CUHK-Shenzhen 2022

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around September for spring.

content.about_text: Peking University is a major public research university located in Beijing, China, and one of the country's oldest and most prominent comprehensive universities. It offers a wide range of programs across the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and professional fields. Most courses are taught in Chinese, with a smaller selection of English-taught courses available and marked accordingly in the course information system.
content.why_yonsei: Peking University is well suited to a Yonsei student who wishes to study in China and engage with Chinese-language instruction, since the majority of undergraduate and master's courses are taught in Chinese; Chinese-taught courses generally require HSK 6 (with each item over 70 points) or an equivalent level, while a set of English-taught courses is also available. A number of units and course types are not open to exchange students, including the Health Science Center, the School of Software and Microelectronics, faculties with special prerequisites, tailor-made and double-degree programs, and the English-taught graduate courses of special master's programs such as the MBA, the Master in International Relations, the Master in Public Policy, the LLM in Chinese Law, and the Yenching Academy. The Shenzhen Graduate School is available only on a case-by-case basis, and Chinese-taught master-level courses require the relevant faculty's permission. Course information for the coming semester is typically published in mid-January or mid-July before registration opens.
content.campus_location: Peking University's main campus is in the Haidian District in northwestern Beijing, set around the historic Yan Yuan grounds with classical gardens, lakes, and traditional architecture within China's capital.

video_links.label: Start Your Journey at Peking University

universities.language_other_notes:

universities.restricted_areas: School of Medicine, Medical School
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around September for spring and around March for fall.

content.about_text: Nanjing University (NJU) is a public research university in Nanjing, Jiangsu, China, established in 1902 and among the oldest and most prestigious universities in the country. It is a member of the elite C9 League, a group of leading Chinese institutions, and offers programs across the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, engineering, and management. Chinese is the primary language of instruction, but a range of content courses is also offered in English.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei students can enroll in English-taught content courses at both undergraduate and graduate levels, drawing on disciplines such as liberal arts, law, business, foreign studies, social and behavioral sciences, chemistry, AI, and earth sciences. English-taught courses generally require IELTS 6 or TOEFL 70 (with a higher TOEFL threshold for some graduate courses), and exchange students are typically expected to take at least three courses per semester. Please note that the School of Medicine and Medical School are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: Nanjing University is based in Nanjing, a historic city in Jiangsu province, with its principal Xianlin and Gulou campuses; the university also operates in Suzhou. Nanjing is a major eastern Chinese city with strong transport links.

video_links.label: Nanjing University (English Introduction) | 南京大学宣传片 蓝光1080P

Fudan provides three different exchange programs. Students must choose ONLY ONE of them:
1. Chinese Language Program (taking Chinese Language courses at
ICES ONLY)
2. General Advanced Program (taking undergraduate-level courses)
In general, General Advanced Program students CAN NOT take Chinese Language courses at ICES. Limited Chinese Language courses at ICES and non-credit Chinese language training classes might be available in this program, but there is NO guarantee that they will be available every semester. Registered Chinese Language courses at ICES
will appear on your official transcript, but non-credit Chinese language training classes will NOT.
Undergraduate-level courses in basic medical sciences, clinical medicine, and nursing are not available.
English-taught Course lists: https://ogp.fudan.edu.cn/16092/list.htm

EMA course lists: https://ogp.fudan.edu.cn/16092/list.htm
Here are the schools/departments that offer EMA courses: Department of Chinese Language and Literature, School of Economics, School of International Relations and Public Affairs, School of Philosophy. Other schools/departments DO NOT offer EMA courses.

content.about_text: Fudan University, founded in 1905, is one of China's most prestigious universities and a member of the elite C9 League, based in Shanghai. It offers exchange students a wide range of English-taught courses alongside Chinese-taught options.
content.why_yonsei: Fudan offers Yonsei students an exchange at a top Chinese university in Shanghai, with a broad selection of English-taught courses; Chinese-taught courses are also possible, though these require HSK 6 with a score above 200. Exchange students choose one of Fudan's exchange-programme tracks. Please note that Education, postgraduate Law, and Music are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: Fudan's main Handan campus sits in the Yangpu district of Shanghai, China's largest and most international city, a global centre for finance, trade, and culture.
