content.about_text: Beijing Normal University (BNU), founded in 1902, is one of China's most prestigious universities and its leading institution for education and teacher training, based in Beijing. It offers exchange students both English-taught courses and Chinese-language study.
content.why_yonsei: BNU offers Yonsei students an exchange in China's capital, Beijing, with English-taught credit courses as well as Chinese-language programmes (no prior Chinese is required for the language programmes). For faculty-level exchange, language requirements are set by the host school, and HSK is not required. Note that Chinese-taught degree programmes have their own admission requirements.
content.campus_location: BNU's main campus is in the Haidian district in the north-west of Beijing, the city's university quarter, close to many of China's leading institutions.

universities.restricted_areas: Law, Medicine, Nursing, Social Work
universities.admission_notes:
To meet this requirement, submit one of the following items directly from the testing agency. 

Official transcript (mark sheet) showing the completion of one year of full-time study at a high school located in the United States
Official statement of completion from one full year of full-time study at a high school where English is the official language of instruction
Official SAT 1 Critical Reading score of 470 (old SAT) OR new SAT reading score of 26. Baylor's SAT code is 6032.
ACT English score of 20. Baylor's ACT code is 4062
Official IELTS score of 6.5 (We will accept IELTS Indicator)
Official TOEFL score of 80 (We will accept TOEFL Home Edition and your TOEFL MyBest Score.) Baylor's TOFEL institution code is 6032.
Official PTE score of 60
Duolingo English Test score of 110
*Students who do not meet the minimum requirement as listed above may be individually considered for admission through a holistic review process.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around February for spring and around February for fall.

content.about_text: Baylor University is a private Christian research university located in Waco, Texas, in the United States. It is a comprehensive institution offering programmes across the arts and sciences, business, engineering, and the professions, with English as the language of instruction. The university's international programmes are coordinated by its Center for Global Engagement.
content.why_yonsei: Baylor University provides Yonsei exchange students an English-medium curriculum across a broad range of undergraduate fields, with exchange admission handled through partner-institution nomination. Incoming students are supported by the Center for Global Engagement and programmes such as the Global Friendship Program, which connects international students with local families. Please note that Law, Medicine, Nursing, and Social Work are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in Waco, a city in central Texas situated along the Brazos River, roughly midway between Dallas and Austin. The setting is a mid-sized Texas city with a traditional campus environment.

universities.available_areas: Engineering Only
universities.restricted_areas: Except Engineering
universities.admission_notes:
Students are highly encouraged to list 3-5 placement preferences in their application. We try to honor every student’s first preference but due to the number of applications received and the availability at each institution, we are not always able to honor the first preference.
If the student is not placed at their first-choice school, this does not mean they were “rejected” from that school. It simply means that, due to a variety of factors, we were unable to accommodate their first-choice request and their application was forwarded to the next available placement preference.
universities.notes: GE3 (Global Engineering Education Exchange) is not a single university but a global consortium of engineering schools administered by the Institute of International Education (IIE), with around 70 member universities in more than 20 countries. It is open to undergraduate engineering students only. Through GE3 you study for a semester or a full year at a member university abroad while continuing to pay tuition at Yonsei. The available host universities and their course offerings vary, so please review the GE3 member list and consult OIA when choosing a destination.

Arts and Sciences, which includes:
American Studies, Anthropology, Applied Science, Art & Art History, Biology, Chemistry, Chinese Language and Culture, Classical Studies, Computer Science, Economics, Education, English Language and Literature, Environmental Science & Policy, Film Studies, Geology, Global Studies (African Studies, East Asian Studies, European Studies, Latin American Studies, Middle East Studies, Russian and Post-Soviet Studies), Government, History, International Relations, Kinesiology (Physical Education), Linguistics, Literary & Cultural Studies, Mathematics, Medieval & Renaissance Studies, Military Science,
Modern Languages and Literatures, Music, Neuroscience, Philosophy, Physics, Psychology, Public Policy, Religious Studies, Sociology, Theater, Speech & Dance, Women’s Studies
universities.restricted_areas: Exchange students cannot take business, law, or graduate level courses.

content.about_text: William & Mary is a public research university in Williamsburg, Virginia, founded in 1693 under a royal charter from King William III and Queen Mary II. It is the second-oldest institution of higher learning in the United States and educated several of the nation's founding figures. The university is known for its strong liberal arts and sciences programs, and English is the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: Exchange students at William & Mary enroll through the Arts and Sciences division and may select from a broad range of subjects, including American Studies, Computer Science, Economics, Government, History, International Relations, and the natural sciences. Please note that exchange students cannot take business, law, or graduate-level courses. Incoming students are supported by the Global Education Office at the Reves Center and may join the Global Friends Program to connect with the local community.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in Williamsburg, a historic colonial-era town in southeastern Virginia. The Reves Center for International Studies, which hosts incoming exchange students, sits on South Boundary Street near the heart of campus.

universities.available_areas:
Exchange students are allowed to take any courses at WWU. However, there are some limitations to enrollment in the following areas: College of Business courses, Environmental Studies, Chemistry, Computer Science, Political Science, Biology, Geology, Physical Education, Kinesiology, Health and Recreation, Communications, English, Psychology, Education and Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages (TESOL) courses. Audition or portfolio is required for admission to some Music and Art classes.

Regarding Physical Education classes: All Physical Education classes have additional charges. This means that the normal exchange tuition waivers provided by WWU will not cover PE classes. So, PE classes will not be an option unless students choose to pay the additional charge per credit fee themselves.
Regarding online classes: Online courses are not available for exchange students. Please do not register for online courses.
There is no guarantee of being able to enroll in all desired courses, so flexibility is required and the courses requested may not be the actual courses taken while at WWU. Course enrollment is often at the discretion of the course instructor and/or academic department, or depends on the space available in any given course. Information about academic departments https://www.wwu.edu/department-directory and courses offered, https://admin.wwu.edu/pls/wwis/wwsktime.SelClass .
universities.restricted_areas:
Exchange students are allowed to take any courses at WWU. However, there are some limitations to enrollment in the following areas: College of Business courses, Environmental Studies, Chemistry, Computer Science, Political Science, Biology, Geology, Physical Education, Kinesiology, Health and Recreation, Communications, English, Psychology, Education and Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages (TESOL) courses. Audition or portfolio is required for admission to some Music and Art classes.
Regarding Physical Education classes: All Physical Education classes have additional charges. This means that the normal exchange tuition waivers provided by WWU will not cover PE classes. So, PE classes will not be an option unless students choose to pay the additional charge per credit fee themselves.
Regarding online classes: Online courses are not available for exchange students. Please do not register for online courses.
There is no guarantee of being able to enroll in all desired courses, so flexibility is required and the courses requested may not be the actual courses taken while at WWU. Course enrollment is often at the discretion of the course instructor and/or academic department, or depends on the space available in any given course. Information about academic departments https://www.wwu.edu/department-directory and courses offered,courses offered: https://wwu.via-trm.com/visitor-programs-v2.
universities.admission_notes: English requirements(others): IELTS 6.5

content.about_text: Western Washington University (WWU), founded in 1893, is a public university in Bellingham, Washington, on the north-west coast of the United States, midway between Seattle and Vancouver, Canada. It runs on a quarter system across the arts, sciences, business, and environmental studies.
content.why_yonsei: WWU offers Yonsei students an English-taught exchange in the scenic Pacific Northwest, with small classes (a student-to-faculty ratio of about 17 to 1) and easy access to mountains, sea, and the cities of Seattle and Vancouver. Exchange students may take courses across most areas, with some enrolment limitations in certain subjects.
content.campus_location: WWU's campus sits in Bellingham, a relaxed coastal city between Bellingham Bay and Mount Baker, with the San Juan Islands offshore and both Seattle and Vancouver within easy reach.

universities.toefl_subscores: 100 or higher preferred particularly if field of study is in humanities or social sciences
universities.available_areas: All Arts & Sciences undergraduate fields are open to exchange students.
universities.restricted_areas: No access to graduate/professional school programs (including Law, Medicine, Social Work) is possible. Arts & Sciences exchange students may have difficulty accessing courses in other undergraduate divisions such as Business, Engineering, or Art/Architecture.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and around January for fall.

content.about_text: Washington University in St. Louis is a private research university in St. Louis, Missouri, in the United States. Incoming exchange students are hosted through its Arts & Sciences division, coordinated by the Overseas Programs office. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei exchange students join through the Arts & Sciences Incoming Exchange Program, where all undergraduate Arts & Sciences fields are open, and an exchange coordinator serves as the academic advisor. Students must enroll in between 12 and 21 units per term to satisfy visa requirements and university rules. Please note that graduate and professional school programs, including Law, Medicine, and Social Work, are not available, and Arts & Sciences exchange students may have difficulty accessing courses in other undergraduate divisions such as Business, Engineering, or Art and Architecture.
content.campus_location: The university is located in St. Louis, Missouri, giving students access to the wider metropolitan area for recreation, cultural offerings, and internships. The campus sits near the city's Forest Park area.

content.about_text: Washington College is a private liberal arts college located in Chestertown, Maryland, in the United States. It is a small, undergraduate-focused institution offering programmes across the humanities, social sciences, natural sciences, and the arts, with instruction in English. The college emphasises close interaction between students and faculty in a residential liberal arts setting.
content.why_yonsei: Washington College offers Yonsei exchange students a small, English-medium liberal arts environment with a wide selection of majors and minors, including American Studies, environmental science, economics, and the humanities. Its Global Education Office coordinates visiting exchange students and aims to respond to completed applications within about four weeks, and students from selected partners may serve as language assistants. The intimate class sizes and residential campus provide a different experience from a large research university.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in Chestertown, a historic town on the Chester River on Maryland's Eastern Shore. It is a small-town setting within reach of the Chesapeake Bay region.

universities.available_areas: Exchange students are permitted to study any areas as long as they meet the pre-requisites for those courses.
universities.restricted_areas:
1. First Year Seminar and Freshman Composition-- these courses are only for degree-seeking students. 
2. Graduate students can only take undergraduate-level courses

content.about_text: Washington & Jefferson College (W&J) is a small private liberal arts college in Washington, Pennsylvania, with roots dating to 1781. It offers a close-knit American liberal arts experience built on small classes and an active campus community of more than 60 student clubs and organisations.
content.why_yonsei: W&J gives Yonsei students a classic small-college liberal arts experience in the United States, with small classes, a broad choice of subjects, and easy access to clubs and sports. Exchange students may take courses across the curriculum provided they meet the prerequisites; a few first-year-only courses, such as the First-Year Seminar and Freshman Composition, are reserved for degree-seeking students.
content.campus_location: W&J's historic campus sits in the small city of Washington, Pennsylvania, about a 40-minute drive south of Pittsburgh, offering a compact, residential setting.

universities.available_areas: https://studyabroad.wisc.edu/exchange/course-enrollment/
universities.restricted_areas: https://studyabroad.wisc.edu/exchange/course-enrollment/
universities.admission_notes: English requirements(Others): IELTS 6.5 or Duolingo 115

content.about_text: The University of Wisconsin-Madison is the flagship public research university of the state of Wisconsin in the United States, founded in 1848. It is a comprehensive university offering a broad range of programmes across the humanities, sciences, engineering, business, and social sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: UW-Madison enrolls exchange students directly into regular English-taught courses alongside degree-seeking students, giving Yonsei students access to a wide curriculum rather than a separate exchange track. Course availability and enrollment are managed through the Office of the Registrar after acceptance, and students should review the exchange course-enrollment guidance, since some departments and courses carry restrictions or prerequisites.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in Madison, the capital of Wisconsin, set along the shores of Lake Mendota and adjacent to the city's downtown.

universities.toefl_subscores: 105 recommended
universities.available_areas:
College of Arts & Sciences Only. https://artsci.washington.edu/all-departments
**Students can take classes outside of the college of arts and sciences (they are admitted through this college but not restricted to these courses). Students will often need to show they have taken similar courses or prerequistes from their home university if there are course requirements for this.
universities.restricted_areas: Undergraduate: Business, Communications, Art and design, Economics, Psychology, Design, and Law, Societies & Justice courses in particular are extremely competitive so you should plan to take courses outside of these areas. Art/Design and Professional/fee-based programs - these courses are unavailable for all incoming exchange students Business and STEM courses (especially Computer Science and Engineering) have some limitations, as UW degree students and departmental exchange students are given priority.
universities.admission_notes: English requirements(others): We also accept the IELTS (minimum 6, recommended 7), and Duolingo (minimum 105, recommedned 120)
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in August for spring and around June for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Washington is a large public research university in Seattle, in the United States, and one of the country's well-established institutions for teaching and research. It hosts more than 200 visiting exchange students each year from partner universities around the world. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei students on a university-wide exchange are admitted through the College of Arts & Sciences, which spans art, humanities, natural sciences, and social sciences. While students are admitted through this college, they are not strictly limited to its courses and may take classes in other areas, though departments may require evidence of relevant prior coursework or prerequisites. Please note that professional and fee-based programs, including the Foster School of Business, Information School, and the Schools of Medicine, Nursing, and Pharmacy, are not open to exchange students, and Business and STEM courses such as Computer Science and Engineering have limited availability because degree students are given priority.
content.campus_location: The university's main campus is in Seattle, a major city in the Pacific Northwest of the United States. The campus is known for its setting near the city and waterfront.

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Rochester is a private research university founded in 1850 and located in Rochester, New York. It is recognized as one of the leading private research universities in the United States, ranking among the top institutions in federally financed science, engineering, and medical research, and offers more than 70 majors. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: The University of Rochester suits Yonsei students who want a smaller, research-focused private university in the United States with flexible, open curriculum options. Instruction is in English, so students must meet the host university's English proficiency requirements. Incoming exchange students study for a semester or a year and are supported by the Center for Education Abroad.
content.campus_location: The campus is situated along the Genesee River in Rochester, a city in upstate New York. The setting provides a compact, walkable environment for exchange students.

useful_links.label: Incoming Exchange Students – Center for Education Abroad

universities.available_areas: Business, Arts & Science areas (English, Biology, Art, etc.), Leadership Studies.
universities.restricted_areas: graduate programs (law, MBA, School of Professional and Continuing Studies); please also note that there may be a small number of course(s) that are only for 1st year or 4th year students. This is determined by each specific department or office and varies from semester to semester. Some of these courses allow 2nd and 3rd year students to enroll if space remains. Course numbers ending in "U" are offered by the School of Continuing Studies. These courses may not meet home institution degree requirements, are not available for undergraduates until arrival at Richmond, and will require special approval for registration. Business exchange students are NOT permitted to take courses in the School of Continuing Studies.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around May for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Richmond is a private liberal arts university founded in 1830 and located in Richmond, Virginia, United States. It is known for participatory academics, small class sizes and a combination of liberal arts and business education, with the language of instruction being English. Incoming exchange students are accepted from partner institutions through University of Richmond International Education.
content.why_yonsei: Richmond offers Yonsei students an English-taught exchange with small classes and close access to faculty. Available areas include Business, Arts and Sciences subjects (such as English, Biology and Art) and Leadership Studies. Please note that graduate programmes (Law, MBA, and the School of Professional and Continuing Studies) are not open to exchange students, and Business exchange students are not permitted to take courses in the School of Continuing Studies. Sufficient English proficiency is required for admission.
content.campus_location: The University of Richmond occupies a roughly 350-acre campus located about six miles from downtown Richmond, Virginia. The setting combines a green, residential campus with proximity to the state capital.

universities.available_areas:
1. Schools & Majors: http://internationalexchanges.pitt.edu/schools
 2. Course Information: http:// internationalexchanges.pitt.edu/courses
universities.admission_notes:
1. See details: http://internationalexchanges.pitt.edu/eligibility
 2. Students who score less than 100
 on the TOEFL iBT or Band 7.0 on the IELTS must take an additional proficiency test upon arrival.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in May for spring and around July for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Pittsburgh (Pitt) is a public research university in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, in the United States, founded in 1787. It is organized into multiple schools and offers a broad range of undergraduate and graduate programs. Instruction is in English.
content.why_yonsei: Pitt gives Yonsei exchange students access to a wide range of schools and majors, with all instruction in English. Incoming exchange students must maintain full-time enrollment of at least 12 credit hours, and students should note that the university does not offer scholarships to incoming exchange students. Students should consult the schools and course information pages to plan their study schedule.
content.campus_location: The main campus is in the Oakland neighborhood of Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, a city in the western part of the state. The urban campus, marked by the landmark Cathedral of Learning, sits within a city known for its universities, healthcare, and technology sectors.

universities.university_name: University of Pennsylvania - Wharton School of Business
universities.available_areas:
1. A nominated student’s major must be within the list of majors in the Wharton School of Business
 See here for a list of majors: https://admissions.upenn.edu/academics/exploring-academics
 2. Some courses require proof of successful completion of pre-requisites or instructor permission to register. Students should work with the appropriate academic advisor or instructor upon arrival at Penn for registration in these courses.
universities.restricted_areas:
• Exchange students are eligible to take undergraduate courses up to the 4999-level.
 • Two of the four courses must be in the admitting college/school. The remaining two
 courses can be within the admitting college/school or in another college/school.
 • If a student takes five courses, three of the five courses need to be within the
 admitting college/school.
 • Students are not permitted to take graduate-level courses OR courses in Penn’s
 Law, Medical, Dental, or Veterinary Schools.
universities.admission_notes: UPenn would accept the TOEFL results within 18 months
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around June for spring and around February for fall.

content.about_text: The Wharton School is the undergraduate and graduate business school of the University of Pennsylvania, an Ivy League institution founded in 1740 in Philadelphia. Established in 1881, Wharton is the world's first collegiate business school and is consistently ranked among the top business schools internationally. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Wharton is suited to Yonsei students whose major falls within the list of majors offered by the Wharton School of Business and who wish to study at a leading United States business school. Exchange students may take undergraduate courses up to the 4999 level, and at least two of four courses (or three of five) must be in the admitting school. Please note that students are not permitted to take graduate-level courses or any courses in Penn's Law, Medical, Dental, or Veterinary Schools, and some courses require proof of completed prerequisites or instructor permission.
content.campus_location: Wharton is part of the University of Pennsylvania's campus on the western edge of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, which covers more than 300 acres of urban landscape.

universities.university_name: University of Pennsylvania - Arts and Sciences/Engineering and Applied Science
universities.available_areas:
1. A nominated student’s major must be within the list of majors in the College of Arts and Sciences or Engineering. 
 See here for a list of majors: https://admissions.upenn.edu/academics/exploring-academics
 2. Some courses require proof of successful completion of pre-requisites or instructor permission to register. Students should work with the appropriate academic advisor or instructor upon arrival at Penn for registration in these courses.
universities.restricted_areas:
• Exchange students are eligible to take undergraduate courses up to the 4999-level.
 • Two of the four courses must be in the admitting college/school. The remaining two
 courses can be within the admitting college/school or in another college/school.

 • Wharton School of Business

• If a student takes five courses, three of the five courses need to be within the
 admitting college/school.
 • Students are not permitted to take graduate-level courses OR courses in Penn’s
 Law, Medical, Dental, or Veterinary Schools.
universities.admission_notes:
UPenn would accept the TOEFL results within 18 months
Students who are not in their leave of absence more than 1 semester before the exchange program.
universities.housing_info: https://residential-services.business-services.upenn.edu/room-selection#Exchange (We do not offer summer housing for calendar year students)
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and around June for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Pennsylvania is a private Ivy League research university in the United States, founded in 1740. This exchange covers its College of Arts and Sciences and the School of Engineering and Applied Science, which together span the humanities, natural and social sciences, and a wide range of engineering disciplines. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Penn offers Yonsei students a place at a leading Ivy League institution, with English-taught undergraduate coursework in arts, sciences, and engineering. A nominated student's major must fall within the list of majors in the College of Arts and Sciences or Engineering, and exchange students may take undergraduate courses up to the 4999 level. Please note that courses in the Wharton School of Business and in Penn's Law, Medical, Dental, and Veterinary Schools, as well as graduate-level courses, are not open to exchange students, and at least half of a student's courses must be within the admitting college.
content.campus_location: The university occupies an urban campus in the University City district of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, close to the city centre and well connected by public transit.

universities.available_areas: Exchange students can take courses both inside and outside their academic major. Popular courses and upper division courses(300-499) may fill up very quickly, so students are encouraged to e flexible with their course selections and register for classes as soon as they can.
universities.restricted_areas: Some courses at the UO have pre-requisite requirements. It will be noted on the course page. Students will need to be able to demonstrate that they have sufficient academic background and preparation to be successful in courses with prerequisites.
universities.admission_notes: Graduate Students:  additional documents may be needed and admission is subject to department approval.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and around June for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Oregon is a public flagship research university founded in 1876 and located in Eugene, Oregon. Classified as a very high research activity institution, it is recognized for programs in areas such as journalism, architecture, environmental law, and sport business, and it is home to the oldest public university honors college in the United States. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: The University of Oregon is a good fit for Yonsei students who want a mid-sized public research university with flexible course access. Exchange students may take courses both inside and outside their academic major, although popular and upper-division (300–499) courses fill quickly, so students are encouraged to remain flexible and register early. Some courses carry prerequisites, and students must be able to demonstrate sufficient academic background to enroll in them.
content.campus_location: The campus sits on roughly 295 acres in Eugene, a city in western Oregon. The university's International Student and Scholar Services office serves as the primary contact point for incoming exchange and visiting students.

useful_links.label: Inbound Exchange & Visiting Students Fact Sheet

universities.available_areas:
Areas of study available to exchange students: College of Arts & Letters, College of Science,
 College of Engineering (CSE + ME limited availability), Keough School of Global Affairs
universities.restricted_areas:
1. Law classes are not available for undergraduate exchange/visiting students.
2. Graduate-level courses (courses numbered 50000+) are not available to undergraduate students.
3. Courses in the Mendoza College of Business are only available to business majors. Business majors are allowed to take a maximum of one course (3 credit hours) at Mendoza.
4. Courses in the following disciplines have limited availability: Aero/Mechanical Engineering and Computer Science Engineering.
5. Architecture students click here for more information on school-specific restrictions
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around May for spring and in June for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Notre Dame, founded in 1842, is a highly regarded private Catholic research university in Notre Dame, Indiana, in the United States. Exchange students study within the College of Arts and Letters, the College of Science, and the College of Engineering.
content.why_yonsei: Notre Dame offers Yonsei students a semester or year on one of America's most iconic campuses, taking courses across the College of Arts and Letters, the College of Science, and (with some limits) Engineering. Please note that Law courses are not available to undergraduate exchange students, and graduate-level courses numbered 50000 and above are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: Notre Dame's landmark campus, with its Golden Dome and lakes, sits just north of the city of South Bend in Indiana, in the American Midwest.

universities.university_name: University of North Carolina at Greensboro
universities.available_areas: Exchange students can take courses in any department. Enrollment wil be subject to availability and will be dependent upon students having met any course pre-requisites. Please refer to the UNC Greensboro catalog for course descriptions, except the following: https://catalog.uncg.edu/courses/
universities.restricted_areas:
Anthropology, Communication Sciences and Disorders, Nursing, Liberal Studies, Counseling and Educational Development, Elementary Education, Educational Research Methodology, Middle Grades Education,
 Speech Pathology & Audiology, Teacher Education, graduate level courses in Accounting, graduate level courses in the English Department, graduate level courses in Psychology, graduate level Consumer, Apparel, and Retail Studies, Graduate level courses in Political
 Science, Graduate level courses in Computer Science, Graduate level courses in Accounting** 

 

**Students wanting graduate accounting classes are encouraged to apply for MBA track. 

 

Students interested in dance (undergraduate only) and music (undergraduate only) are asked to submit a 3-5 minute video so they can be properly placed in the correct course.  Students interested in interior architecture and studio art are asked to provide a
 portfolio, in addition to their application.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around June for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: The University of North Carolina at Greensboro (UNCG) is a public research university located in Greensboro, North Carolina, in the United States. Founded in 1891, it serves more than 19,000 students and offers a broad range of programmes, with English as the language of instruction. The university has more than 100 exchange partners.
content.why_yonsei: UNCG allows Yonsei exchange students to take courses in most departments, subject to availability and prerequisites. Please note that a number of fields are not available, including Anthropology, Communication Sciences and Disorders, Nursing, Liberal Studies, several Education departments, and Speech Pathology and Audiology, along with various graduate-level courses (such as Accounting, English, Psychology, Political Science, and Computer Science). Students interested in dance or music (undergraduate only) are asked to submit a short audition video, and those interested in interior architecture or studio art must provide a portfolio.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in Greensboro, in the Piedmont Triad region of central North Carolina.

universities.university_name: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
universities.available_areas:
Exchange students and Global Visiting Students can take courses in any department within the College of
 Arts and Sciences, which includes more than 40 academic departments and interdisciplinary curricula.
 Enrollment will be subject to availability and will be dependent upon students having met any course prerequisites.
 Please refer to the UNC catalog for course descriptions, http://www.catalog.unc.edu/.
 Note: It is unlikely that students may take full course load within a single department. There is a 2 course limit, per semester, for Economics and Psychology courses.
universities.restricted_areas: Exchange students and Global Visiting Students cannot take courses certain departments or the professional schools unless their home university has a specific agreement with that school. These include: Eshelman School of Pharmacy, Gillings School of Global Public Health, Kenan-Flagler Business School, Law School, School of Dentistry, School of Education, School of Government, School of Information & Library Science, Hussman Schoo of Journalism and Media, School of Medicine, School of Nursing, School of Social Work, Shuford Program in Entrepreneurship, Biomedical Engineeing
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill (UNC) is a public research university in Chapel Hill, North Carolina, in the United States, and one of the oldest public universities in the country. Its College of Arts and Sciences includes more than 40 academic departments and interdisciplinary curricula. Instruction is in English.
content.why_yonsei: UNC offers Yonsei exchange students courses across more than 40 departments within the College of Arts and Sciences, all taught in English, subject to availability and any course prerequisites. Please note there is a limit of two courses per semester in Economics and in Psychology, and it is unlikely that a full course load can be taken within a single department. Exchange students cannot take courses in the professional schools and certain departments, including the Kenan-Flagler Business School, the Law School, and the Schools of Medicine, Nursing, Pharmacy, Public Health, Education, and others, unless a specific agreement exists, so students should plan their courses within the College of Arts and Sciences.
content.campus_location: The university is located in Chapel Hill, North Carolina, part of the Research Triangle region. The setting is a classic college town with an established campus and a strong research environment.

universities.toefl_subscores: Listening and Reading: 23, Writing and Speaking: 21
universities.available_areas:
Incoming exchange students are only permitted to take courses through the College of Literature, Science, and the Arts(LSA); https://lsa.umich.edu/lsa/academics/courses.html
Incoming exchange students are not permitted to take courses in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS). 

Incoming exchange students are encouraged to stay in UM on-campus housing. Unfortunately, UM on-campus housing is not guaranteed and it is extremely difficult for exchange students to secure a spot in on-campus housing. Interested students should apply for UM on-campus housing, but should also look for off-campus housing options in the event that they cannot secure on-campus housing. 

Only undergraduate students may apply for the exchange program.
universities.restricted_areas:
In general, exchange students should plan on selecting undergraduate coursework from the College of Literature, Science, and Arts (LSA). Access to courses in other units is severely limited and requires special permission and overrides, which may not be approved.
 Qualified students apply to the College of Literature, Science, and the Arts and may enroll in courses from any department in the college. Courses in Economics, Computer Science, Communication Studies, Psychology, and Political Science are particularly difficult to enroll in. CGIS cannot guarantee enrollment in any specific courses, especially from these four departments. Therefore, students should not depend on enrollment in courses primarily from these departments.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in May for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Michigan is a public research university founded in 1817 and located in Ann Arbor, Michigan. It is one of the foremost research universities in the United States, comprising nineteen colleges and offering degree programs across some 250 disciplines, and the language of instruction is English. Incoming exchange students are hosted through the College of Literature, Science, and the Arts (LSA) via the Center for Global and Intercultural Study (CGIS).
content.why_yonsei: The University of Michigan offers Yonsei undergraduate students access to one of the strongest public research universities in the United States. Please note that incoming exchange students may take courses only through the College of Literature, Science, and the Arts (LSA) and are not permitted to enroll in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS); access to other units is severely limited and requires special permission. Courses in Economics, Computer Science, Communication Studies, Psychology, and Political Science are particularly difficult to enroll in and cannot be guaranteed, the program is open only to undergraduate students, and on-campus housing is not guaranteed.
content.campus_location: The university is based in Ann Arbor, Michigan, with its historic Central Campus and a separate North Campus among more than 580 buildings. Exchange students often live in residence halls but should also research off-campus housing options early.

universities.available_areas: 180 Bachelor degree programs offered. Students may select 100-level through 400-level classes for which they meet the pre-requisite requirements. Students can only enroll in 500-level classes with special permission of the instructor and department.
universities.restricted_areas:
1. Course Enrollment and Restrictions: https://acrobat.adobe.com/link/track?uri=urn:aaid:scds:US:32aafa65-642f-36f0-abc0-a2deb2d81a91
2. Graduate students can participate in exchange. It should be noted that, similar to the undergraduate exchange, students are restricted to taking undergraduate level courses. Like undergraduate exchange, students can take a minimum of 12 credits and a maximum of 20 credits. 
3. Students cannot enroll in Music performance, Medical School, or Law School courses. Students coming on the undergraduate exchange are also not allowed to take 600-level classes. Special restrictions apply to courses within the College of Engineering and Miami Herbert Business School. Neuroscience classes may not be available to exchange students due to limited space.
4. Moreover, students cannot take more than two courses per Faculty(subject) in the School of Business. For example, a student if a student is enrolled in MKT 100 and MKT 200, the student cannot enroll in a Third MKT course.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in May for spring and in May for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Miami is a private research university in the United States, with its main campus in Coral Gables, Florida. Founded in 1925, it offers around 180 bachelor degree programmes across a wide range of disciplines, with English as the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: Miami offers Yonsei exchange students a broad selection of 100-level through 400-level courses for which they meet the prerequisites; 500-level courses require special permission of the instructor and department. Please note that Music performance, Medical School, and Law School courses are not open to exchange students. Special restrictions apply within the College of Engineering and the Miami Herbert Business School, students may take no more than two courses per subject in the School of Business, and Neuroscience courses may be limited by space. Graduate exchange students are restricted to undergraduate-level courses.
content.campus_location: The main campus is in Coral Gables, just south of the city of Miami, Florida, and includes a theatre, an arboretum, and an architecture centre.

universities.available_areas:
https://www.umass.edu/international-programs/international-students-scholars/exchange-students/study-options
All UMass programs are open to exchange students but the ones listed in the restricted study area.
universities.restricted_areas:
Undergraduate students are not eligible to take courses in the following departments:
Computer Science
Dance
Nursing
Studio Art
Music*

*Registration for classes in this department is contingent upon completing on-campus auditions.

 
Undergraduate students are only permitted to enroll in classes within the following specific schools if they have officially declared a major within that school at their home university.
Isenberg School of Management (Accounting, Finance, Hospitality & Tourism Management, Management, Marketing, Operations & Information Management, and Sport Management majors)
School of Engineering (Chemical, Civil & Environmental, Electrical & Computer, and Mechanical & Industrial)
universities.admission_notes:
Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL): minimum score of 80*
 International English Language Testing System (IELTS): minimum score of 6.5*
 Pearson Test of English (PTE Academic): minimum overall score of 53*
 Duolingo English Test: minimum overall score of 105*
 Native English Speakers: Note from home institution stating language abilities.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Massachusetts Amherst is a public land-grant research university in Amherst, Massachusetts, founded in 1863 as an agricultural college. It is the flagship and largest campus of the University of Massachusetts system and a member of the Five College Consortium alongside Amherst, Smith, Mount Holyoke, and Hampshire colleges. English is the language of instruction, and the university offers a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs.
content.why_yonsei: UMass Amherst is the flagship public research university of Massachusetts and welcomes Yonsei exchange students into an English-taught environment with a broad course selection across its schools and colleges. Please note several restrictions for undergraduate exchange students: courses in Computer Science, Dance, Nursing, Studio Art, and Music are not open (Music also requires an on-campus audition), and enrollment in the Isenberg School of Management or the College of Engineering is limited to students who have officially declared a relevant major at their home university. Students should plan their course choices around these restricted areas.
content.campus_location: The University of Massachusetts Amherst is located in the town of Amherst in the Pioneer Valley of western Massachusetts. The setting is a large residential campus within the Five College area.

universities.toefl_subscores: minimum sectional scores of 22
universities.available_areas: All areas are available for study for Yonsei students except for courses in the Smith School of Business, but please note limited enrollment programs may have restrictions (computer science, psychology, etc.)
universities.restricted_areas: Limited Enrollment Programs: Neuroscience, Biological Sciences, Biochemistry, Chemistry, & Environmental Sciences and Policy-Biodiversity and Conservation Business (only business majors from institutions that have a specific exchange agreement with the Smith School of Business may pre-register for business classes) Communication Computer Science (priority is given to students who are Computer Science majors at their home institution. Students cannot enroll for more than two, maybe 3 CS classes per semester) Criminology & Criminal Justice Economics (priority is given to students who are Economics majors at their home institutions) Engineering (only engineering majors from institutions that have a specific exchange agreement with the A. James Clark School of Engineering may take engineering classes) Government & Politics Information Studies Journalism Psychology, Business
universities.admission_notes: Must have completed a minimum of 2 semesters of university before studying at UMD

content.about_text: The University of Maryland, College Park is the flagship public research university of the state of Maryland in the United States. It is organized into twelve colleges and schools and offers a broad range of programmes across the arts, sciences, engineering, and business. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Maryland gives Yonsei exchange students access to thousands of English-taught courses across its colleges, and incoming students typically take twelve to twenty credits per semester. Please note that courses in the Robert H. Smith School of Business are generally not open to exchange students unless their home institution has a specific agreement with the school. Several limited-enrollment programs, including Computer Science, Psychology, Economics, Communication, and Engineering, carry restrictions or priority rules, so course access in these areas is not guaranteed.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in College Park, Maryland, just outside Washington, D.C., placing students close to the capital's institutions, museums, and transit.

universities.available_areas: Students may choose courses from over 70 fields of study. Visit https://courses.illinois.edu/ to view the course schedule.
universities.restricted_areas:
Please remind your students that Illinois cannot guarantee enrollment in any specific course, so it is important for exchange students to be as flexible as possible in their course selection.
 It is not possible to take courses offered by the College of Business and College of Law.
 Exchange students may take some courses in the College of Engineering, but courses in certain departments such as Computer Science are usually restricted. We cannot guarantee enrollment in any specific course. It is important for students to be flexible in their course selection. For a list of closed and limited programs, go to: https://studyabroad.illinois.edu/incoming-students/incoming-application-process/restricted-courses/.
universities.admission_notes: Students whose TOEFL is less than 80 will be required to take the English Placement Test upon arrival. Based on the outcome of the test, certain English language courses may be recommended for the student. (Intensive English Institute information at http://www.iei.uiuc.edu/). Proposed program may have additional admission criteria.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign is a public land-grant research university in the United States and the flagship campus of the University of Illinois system. It is a large institution offering courses across more than 70 fields of study, with English as the language of instruction. It is widely recognised for its programmes in engineering, the sciences, and many other disciplines.
content.why_yonsei: Illinois offers Yonsei exchange students a broad choice of courses across over 70 fields. Please note that courses in the College of Business and the College of Law are not available to exchange students. Some College of Engineering courses are open, but certain departments such as Computer Science are usually restricted. Illinois cannot guarantee enrolment in any specific course, so students should keep their course selection flexible and consult the list of closed and limited programs.
content.campus_location: The campus spans the neighbouring cities of Urbana and Champaign in east-central Illinois, forming a large university town setting.

universities.available_areas: O
universities.restricted_areas: Students can find the updated list of restricted study areas on our Academics page: https://manoa.hawaii.edu/mix/inbound/academics/
universities.admission_notes:
1. Students scoring less than TOEFL iBT 100 must take the ELI Placement Test upon arrival and may need to take between 1-3 courses in the ELI depending on their placemnet exam score.
2. Application steps for Graduate students: https://manoa.hawaii.edu/mix/inbound/how-to-apply-postgraduate-students/
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Hawaii at Manoa (UHM) is the flagship campus of the University of Hawaii system, a public research university located in Honolulu, Hawaii, United States. Its international exchange programmes are administered by the Manoa International Exchange (MIX) office, which welcomes incoming exchange students from partner universities worldwide. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: UHM offers Yonsei students an English-taught exchange in a distinctive setting that combines a comprehensive United States research university with Pacific and Asian cultural influences. Incoming exchange students do not pay tuition to UHM and may study for one or two semesters. Please note that some study areas are restricted; students should review the current list of restricted areas on the MIX Academics page before selecting courses.
content.campus_location: The Manoa campus sits in the Manoa Valley of Honolulu on the island of Oahu, a short distance from the city centre and the coast. The setting offers a green, valley campus within a major Pacific city.

universities.available_areas: Most study areas available! But courses in Terry College of Business, SPIA School of Public and International Affairs, and Grady College of Journalism & Mass Communication have some restrictions.
universities.restricted_areas: Students are not permitted to take courses in Law, Pharmacy, and Veterinary Medicine
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in May for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Georgia (UGA) is a public land-grant research university in Athens, Georgia, in the United States. Chartered in 1785, it is the nation's oldest state-chartered university and offers more than 130 study abroad and exchange programs across most of its academic departments. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: UGA gives a Yonsei exchange student access to most study areas in English at a historic public research university, though several fields carry limits that students must observe. Courses in the Terry College of Business, the School of Public and International Affairs, and the Grady College of Journalism and Mass Communication have some restrictions, and students are not permitted to take courses in Law, Pharmacy, or Veterinary Medicine. Students should confirm course availability after admission.
content.campus_location: The university occupies a 759-acre campus in Athens, a classic college town in the northeastern part of the state of Georgia. Athens lies about 110 kilometres east of Atlanta.

universities.available_areas: College of Arts & Sciences, College of Engineering, Business
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and in March for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Florida is a public land-grant research university founded in 1853 and located in Gainesville, Florida. It is the oldest and one of the largest universities in the state, with an enrollment of roughly 50,000 students, and is a member of the Association of American Universities. Business, engineering, and the social sciences are among its most popular fields, and the language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: The University of Florida is a strong choice for Yonsei students seeking a large public research university in the United States, with exchange coursework available in the College of Arts and Sciences, the College of Engineering, and Business. All instruction is in English, so students must meet the host university's English proficiency requirements as confirmed at nomination. Incoming exchange students are nominated by their home institution and complete the UFIC online application by the posted deadlines.
content.campus_location: The university occupies a large tree-lined campus in Gainesville, a city in north-central Florida about 115 kilometers southwest of Jacksonville. The campus includes a lake, a wildlife sanctuary, and art and natural history museums.

useful_links.label: UF International Center – Exchange Visitor Services

universities.toefl_subscores: Minimum of 19 in each subsection
universities.available_areas: College of Agriculture & Natural Resources, Enginering, Liberal Arts & Sciences, Business (courses are restricted to Business majors only), Fine Arts & Statistics (limited course availability)
universities.restricted_areas: Nursing, Pharmacy, Law, Social Work, Medicine, Education courses are not available
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Connecticut (UConn) is a public research university founded in 1881 and located in Storrs, Connecticut, United States. It is organised across 14 schools and colleges that together offer more than 100 degree programmes to over 30,000 students. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: A Yonsei exchange student at UConn may take courses in the College of Agriculture and Natural Resources, Engineering, Liberal Arts and Sciences, Fine Arts, and Statistics (with limited availability), as well as Business courses, which are restricted to Business majors only. Please note that Nursing, Pharmacy, Law, Social Work, Medicine, and Education courses are not available to exchange students. UConn is consistently ranked among the top public universities in the United States.
content.campus_location: UConn's main campus is in the town of Storrs in rural eastern Connecticut, offering a traditional residential American campus setting. Hartford and other regional cities are within driving distance.

universities.toefl_subscores: Undergraduate: 83, Graduate:90
universities.available_areas: https://reciprocity.uceap.universityofcalifornia.edu/plan-your-studies/uc-courses
universities.restricted_areas: https://reciprocity.uceap.universityofcalifornia.edu/plan-your-studies/uc-courses/major-preparation
universities.admission_notes:
*Bachelor’s degree transfer applicants are not eligible to apply.

content.about_text: The University of California (UC) is one of the world's leading public university systems, with nine undergraduate campuses across California — among them Berkeley, Los Angeles, San Diego, Davis, Irvine, and Santa Barbara. Yonsei students join through the UC Education Abroad Program (UCEAP) Reciprocity exchange.
content.why_yonsei: UC offers Yonsei students an English-taught exchange at one of nine California campuses through UCEAP Reciprocity, with access to the same courses and services as UC students and a UC transcript at the end. Students usually take 14 to 17 units per term and may begin in the fall (August/September) or the January term, but not the spring quarter. Please note that "impacted" (very popular) courses and some majors may be closed to reciprocity students, who are placed after UC degree-seeking students for those classes.
content.campus_location: The UC system spans nine undergraduate campuses across California, from the San Francisco Bay Area and the Central Valley to Los Angeles, San Diego, and the coast — offering a wide range of settings from large urban campuses to seaside college towns.

universities.university_name: University at Albany, State University of New York
universities.available_areas: all except honor's college and some business classes are restricted

content.about_text: The University at Albany is a public research university in Albany, New York, and part of the State University of New York (SUNY) system. Founded in 1844, it is New York State's oldest publicly founded university and serves more than 17,000 students, including international students from over 90 countries. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Located in New York's capital region, the University at Albany offers Yonsei exchange students a broad English-taught curriculum at an established public research university. Most subject areas are open to exchange students, though the Honors College and some Business courses are restricted. Students should confirm course access in the relevant departments after admission.
content.campus_location: The university is based in Albany, the capital of New York State, in the Hudson Valley region of the northeastern United States. The city offers a mid-sized urban setting within reach of New York City, Boston, and Montreal.

universities.toefl_subscores: subscores of 17
universities.available_areas:
Students may take courses in all disciplines provided they meet the equivalent pre-requisite courses. Instructions on search classes is available here: https://www.towson.edu/academics/international/isso/students/exchange/courses.html. 

Students from partner institutions who do not meet the minimum language requirements to study at Towson on exchange may take courses at our English Language Center (ELC) prior to their semester or full year on exchange. This is called the ELC + International Exchange program. Attending the ELC for one or more semesters allows them to improve proficiency in reading, writing and speaking in preparation for regular university courses on exchange.  To apply simply select the ELC + International Exchange option on the International Exchange Student Application.
universities.restricted_areas: Education and nursing can be limited.
universities.admission_notes: Official academic record/transcript, financial documentation with financial support guarentee for students with a financial sponsor, and photocopy of biographical information page of passport must be submitted by exchange coordinator at home university. Student must also submit their online application: https://www.towson.edu/academics/international/isso/students/exchange/application.html
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around March for spring and in March for fall.

content.about_text: Towson University is a public university located in Towson, Maryland, approximately eight miles north of the city of Baltimore. It is the second largest university in Maryland and hosts over 400 international students from more than 80 countries. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Exchange students at Towson typically enroll in four or five classes for a total of 12 to 15 credits per semester and may take courses across most disciplines, provided they meet the equivalent prerequisites. Students who do not meet the minimum language requirements may take courses at the English Language Center prior to their exchange through the ELC + International Exchange program. Please note that course availability in Education and Nursing may be limited for exchange students.
content.campus_location: Towson University sits in the suburban community of Towson, just north of Baltimore, giving students access to a large American university campus while remaining close to a major city. The greater Baltimore-Washington area is within easy reach.

universities.available_areas: utx.global/before-apply
universities.restricted_areas:
https://global.utexas.edu/isss/advising-services/student-exchange-program/before-you-apply

***Students are not allowed for Engineering majors.
universities.admission_notes:
1. Students should complete at least 2 semesters before starting exchange
2. Engineering, McCombs School of Business, Law, Medical School are restricted.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Texas at Austin is a major public research university in Austin, Texas, with 18 colleges and schools, more than 51,000 students, and over 170 undergraduate fields of study.
content.why_yonsei: UT Austin offers Yonsei students a fully English-taught exchange in the vibrant Texan capital of Austin, choosing from a vast range of subjects across its colleges (a full load is 12 credit hours per semester on the general exchange), with no tuition charged to UT Austin. Please note that some colleges and high-demand courses have enrolment restrictions for general-exchange students, so check the exchange information when planning your courses.
content.campus_location: UT Austin's large campus sits just north of downtown Austin, the capital of Texas — a fast-growing city known for live music, technology, and a famously lively student scene.

video_links.label: UT Student Exchange Programs | Texas Global

universities.university_name: The University of Texas at Arlington
universities.available_areas:
Students are eligible to take any course for which they have met the necessary pre-requisites.
 UTA has 9 different colleges and over 50 subject areas(http://catalog.uta.edu/aboututa/programs/) available for study.
universities.admission_notes: TOEFL score most be sent directly to UTA from the Testing Service in order to be considered official.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around April for spring and in June for fall.

content.about_text: The University of Texas at Arlington (UTA) is a public research university in Arlington, Texas, in the United States, and part of the University of Texas System. It teaches in English and is organized into nine colleges spanning more than 50 subject areas. The university supports incoming exchange students through its Office of International Education.
content.why_yonsei: Instruction is in English, and exchange students are generally eligible to take any course for which they have met the necessary prerequisites across UTA's nine colleges and broad subject offering. The input lists no field restrictions for this exchange. Students should confirm course availability and prerequisites with UTA Study Abroad as part of the application process.
content.campus_location: UTA is located in Arlington, Texas, in the Dallas-Fort Worth metropolitan area, one of the largest urban regions in the United States. The campus offers on-campus housing and a full range of student facilities.

useful_links.label: Study Abroad: Office of International Education

universities.available_areas: Fields within Ohio State's College of Arts and Sciences (https://artsandsciences.osu.edu/)
universities.restricted_areas: Engineering, Business, Education, Pharmacy, Social Work, Public Health, Agriculture, Design
universities.admission_notes: We also accept the IELTS and Duolingo tests of English proficiency. IELTS minimum: 6.5; Duolingo minimum: 120
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in May for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The Ohio State University is a large public research university located in Columbus, Ohio, in the United States. It is one of the largest universities in the country, with English as the language of instruction across its colleges. The university's Office of International Affairs manages its student exchange agreements and incoming exchange students.
content.why_yonsei: Instruction is in English, and Yonsei exchange students study on a short-term, non-degree basis for one semester or a full academic year. For this exchange, course selection is centered on fields within Ohio State's College of Arts and Sciences. Please note that Engineering, Business, Education, Pharmacy, Social Work, Public Health, Agriculture, and Design are not open to exchange students under this agreement.
content.campus_location: The main campus is in Columbus, the capital of Ohio, a large Midwestern city in the United States. The campus is extensive and self-contained, with comprehensive academic, athletic, and student facilities.

useful_links.label: Exchange Students: Office of International Affairs

universities.available_areas: temple.edu/courses
universities.restricted_areas: Architecture; Computer & Information Sciences; Business; Education; Performing Arts (Dance, Music, Theater); Professional Schools (Dentistry, Law, Medicine, Pharmacy, Podiatry); Visual Arts (Film/Media Arts, Graphic Design, Fine Arts, etc.); Sport, Tourism, and Hospitality Management
universities.admission_notes:
1. Undergraduate students must have been enrolled at home university for at least 1 academic year; graduate students must receive approval by receiving department at Temple University
 2. English Proficiency Certificate: TOEFL iBT 79 or IELTS 6.0 or Duolingo 110
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around April for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: Temple University is a public research university founded in 1884 and based in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in the United States. It is one of Pennsylvania's state-related research universities and enrolls more than 37,000 students across numerous schools and colleges, with English as the language of instruction. The university is known for its urban setting and broad range of liberal arts and professional programs.
content.why_yonsei: All instruction is in English, which makes Temple accessible to Yonsei exchange students who meet the host university's language requirements. Exchange students study within Temple's College of Liberal Arts and other open units and continue to pay tuition to Yonsei rather than to Temple. Please note that a number of fields are not open to exchange students here, including Architecture, Computer and Information Sciences, Business, Education, Performing Arts, the professional schools (Dentistry, Law, Medicine, Pharmacy, Podiatry), Visual Arts, and Sport, Tourism, and Hospitality Management; students should confirm course availability before applying.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in North Philadelphia, within a large city on the United States East Coast that offers extensive cultural, historical, and transport connections. Philadelphia sits between New York and Washington, D.C., on the regional rail corridor.

Smith College of Engineering & Computer Science, Falk College of Sport & Human Dynamics, the School of Information Studies, Whitman School of Management, and the College of Visual & Performing Arts, although some classes are restricted in VPA.
universities.restricted_areas: School of Architecture, Newhouse Public Communications, Whitman is limited enrollment. universities.admission_notes:

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around December for fall.

content.about_text: Syracuse University is a private research university located in Syracuse, New York, in the United States, comprising thirteen schools and colleges. It offers a wide range of undergraduate programs across fields including arts and sciences, education, engineering and computer science, sport and human dynamics, information studies, management, and visual and performing arts. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Syracuse offers a Yonsei exchange student a broad, English-taught curriculum across many colleges, and exchange students pay no tuition to Syracuse, receiving a tuition grant for each semester, though certain non-tuition fees still apply. Open colleges include Arts and Sciences, Education, the College of Engineering and Computer Science, the Falk College of Sport and Human Dynamics, the School of Information Studies, the Whitman School of Management, and the College of Visual and Performing Arts, although some Visual and Performing Arts classes are restricted. Please note that the School of Architecture and the Newhouse School of Public Communications are not open, Whitman has limited enrollment, and the College of Visual and Performing Arts is open only to students in professional degree programs and is subject to a portfolio review prior to acceptance. Exchange students must obtain an F-1 visa, processed through the university's Center for International Services.
content.campus_location: Syracuse University sits on a hillside campus in the city of Syracuse, in central New York State, about an hour by plane from New York City.

universities.available_areas:
1. College of Arts & Sciences: Advertising & Public Relations, American Studies, Art & Design, Arts Administration, Asian Studies, Biology, Chemistry & Biochemistry, Black Studies, Classics, Communcation/Journalism, Computer Science, Creative Writing, Economics, English, Education Studies, Environment & Sustainability, Fine Arts, Forensic Science, Government, Graphic Design, History, Interior Design, Law, Mathmatics, Media and Film, Philosophy, Physics, Politics, Philosophy & Economics, Psychoogy, Radiation Science, Religious Studies, Sociology, Theater, Women's & Gender Studies
 2. Sawyer Business School
universities.restricted_areas: Media and Film studies
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around September for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: Suffolk University is a private university in Boston, Massachusetts, founded in 1906 and originally established as a law school. It offers undergraduate and graduate programs through its College of Arts and Sciences, Sawyer Business School, and Law School, with a strong emphasis on experiential learning. English is the language of instruction, and the university is known for its programs in business, legal studies, communication, and public policy.
content.why_yonsei: Suffolk University is an English-taught institution in central Boston, giving Yonsei exchange students access to a wide range of liberal arts and business courses across the College of Arts and Sciences and the Sawyer Business School. Its downtown location places students close to the city's government, legal, and financial districts, supporting experiential learning. Please note that Media and Film studies is not open to exchange students; please select courses from the other available areas.
content.campus_location: Suffolk University occupies an urban campus integrated into downtown Boston's Beacon Hill neighborhood. Its academic buildings and residence halls sit steps from the Massachusetts State House and Boston Common.

universities.available_areas: All except restricted -https://www.stonybrook.edu/sb/bulletin/current/courses/browse/byabbreviation/

To meet the minimum course load, business major students will be require to also select courses from other academic departments on campus.
4. Restricted courses: Health-sciences and Pharmacology subjects and Writing 101 & 102, CSE 316, CSE 320, CSE 416
