universities.admission_notes:
1. cannot accept US Citizens, Dual US Citizens or US Green Card Holders into the exchange program
2. Graduate students are limited to 6 credits of graduate classes.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: Stony Brook University is a public research university in the State University of New York (SUNY) system, founded in 1957. Located on Long Island in New York State, it is recognized as one of the system's flagship research institutions, with notable strengths in the sciences, engineering, mathematics, and the health sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: All instruction is in English, so Yonsei exchange students can integrate directly into the curriculum, and the university offers strong programs across the sciences, engineering, and the humanities. Please note that several areas are closed or restricted for exchange students: Health Science programs, Education, and graduate-level study are not open, and Pharmacology, Writing 101 and 102, and certain Computer Science courses (CSE 316, CSE 320, CSE 416) are restricted. Computer Science/Information Systems and Accounting/Business courses are each capped at two courses per semester, and courses in Theatre Arts, Dance, and some languages are limited, so students should plan their study list accordingly.
content.campus_location: The main campus sits in the hamlet of Stony Brook on the North Shore of Long Island, roughly a 90-minute train ride east of New York City. It is a large suburban campus with extensive science and research facilities.

universities.available_areas: Although Randolph-Macon College will make every effort to assist exchange students with meeting their course needs, Randolph-Macon College cannot guarantee the availability of specific courses or seats within courses. Therefore, it is the sole responsibility of the exchange student and his/her home institution to ensure what the student’s graduation requirements at his/her home institution are and understand that those requirements may not be met during the time enrolled at R-MC.
universities.restricted_areas:
Field work placement for education courses cannot be guaranteed for international students as many counties require that anyone working in a K-12 setting (even volunteer student teachers) are US Citizens or legal residents and a background check is required. If your students are interested in EDUC courses that include field work, please advise them accordingly.  
If students are going to study Business at RMC, they will need to bring a computer that can run the Microsoft 365 suite (Note: Chromebooks will not handle the software for the Business courses).

content.about_text: Randolph-Macon College, founded in 1830, is a residential undergraduate liberal arts college in Ashland, Virginia, in the United States, known for small classes and a broad liberal arts curriculum.
content.why_yonsei: Randolph-Macon offers Yonsei students a small-college American liberal arts experience in Virginia, with small classes and a wide choice of subjects on a close-knit residential campus. Please note that field-work placements for education courses cannot be guaranteed for exchange students.
content.campus_location: The college's campus is in the small town of Ashland, Virginia, just north of the state capital, Richmond, and within a couple of hours of Washington, D.C.

universities.toefl_subscores: No section score below 20
universities.available_areas:
Students admitted to Purdue as undergraduates may take courses numbered from 10000 to 49999. Courses numbered 50000 to 59999 require special permission from the department; those numbered 60000 and above are open only to exchange students who have been admitted as graduate students. 
 
 It's very important to read the information about course selection and registration BEFORE you complete that section of the exchange student application.
universities.restricted_areas: Aviation, Education, Nursing, Pharmacy, and Vet Med
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around July for spring and in August for fall.

content.about_text: Purdue University is a public research university whose main campus is located in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States. It is known for its strengths in engineering, science, technology, and agriculture, and it enrols a large student body of more than 50,000 students on the West Lafayette campus. English is the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei exchange students admitted as undergraduates may generally take courses numbered from 10000 to 49999, with higher-numbered courses requiring departmental permission or graduate admission, so reading the course selection guidance before applying is important. Teaching is in English across a wide range of disciplines. Please note that Aviation, Education, Nursing, Pharmacy, and Veterinary Medicine are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The main campus is in West Lafayette, Indiana, a college town along the Wabash River. Indianapolis International Airport is about 70 miles away and Chicago O'Hare is about 140 miles away, both connected by shuttle services.

universities.toefl_subscores: Undergraduate: 16 on each section, Graduate: 18 on each section
universities.available_areas: All normally offered subjects.
universities.restricted_areas: There is no official restrictions except that some subjects are more limited than others and that exchange students cannot take classes that award a degree or certificate.
universities.admission_notes:
1. IELTS: 6.5
Note: While COVID continues to affect testing centers, the IELTS Indicator Test is currently accepted by OSU for graduate level applicants, but not for undergraduate level applicants
DET (Duolingo English Test): 110 overall score for both undergraduates and graduates
2. Some graduate departments may require higher English proficiency scores and/or additional materials. Students are responsible for researching the possibility of additional requirements.
universities.fall_nomination_deadline: The earlier the better in March
universities.spring_nomination_deadline: The earlier the better in October
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around June for fall.

content.about_text: Oregon State University is a public land-grant research university in Corvallis, Oregon, in the United States. Founded in 1868, it is the state's largest university, with on-campus enrollment near 32,000 students and more than 200 undergraduate degree programs. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Oregon State offers Yonsei exchange students access to nearly all normally offered subjects, taught in English, with particular depth in the sciences and engineering. There are no formal field restrictions, although some subjects are more limited than others and exchange students cannot enroll in classes that award a degree or certificate. Incoming exchange placements are administered through IE3 Global.
content.campus_location: The campus is located in Corvallis, a college town in Oregon's Willamette Valley in the Pacific Northwest. The setting offers ready access to both the coast and the Cascade Range.

universities.available_areas: Colleges of Arts & Letters, Business, Science, Education, Health Science, and Engineering all offer strong programs. Link to find classes online: https://courses.odu.edu/
universities.restricted_areas: Nusing
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around March for fall.

content.about_text: Old Dominion University is a public research university in Norfolk, Virginia, in the United States. Established in 1930 and granted university status in 1969, it is classified as an R1 doctoral research institution and enrolls roughly 23,000 students across seven colleges. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: With instruction in English and a broad academic offering, Old Dominion is well suited to a Yonsei exchange student seeking a comprehensive United States university experience. The Colleges of Arts and Letters, Business, Sciences, Education, Health Sciences, and Engineering all offer strong programs, and available classes can be reviewed in advance through the public course catalogue. Please note that Nursing is not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The main campus occupies about 250 acres in Norfolk, a coastal city in the Hampton Roads region of southeastern Virginia. Norfolk sits on the Chesapeake Bay near the Atlantic coast.

useful_links.label: Office of Study Abroad - Program Page

universities.toefl_subscores: Undergraduate: 68, Graduate: 79
universities.available_areas: Course Catalog: http://registrar.okstate.edu/Course-Descriptions
universities.restricted_areas:
1. Not Available: Accounting(except for introductory course, ACCT 2103 Financial Accounting), Computer Science (MSIS-Management Science & Info Systems are available), Some master's level courses, Mechnical & Aerospace Engineering, School of Veterinary Medicine courses, Agricultural Communication
2. Limited Availability: Architecture(very selective! students must submit portfolio of their work and a complete transcript translated into English with course descriptions), Studio Art, Communications (Mass Communications, Multimedia Journalism, and Strategic Communicatoins), Design, Housing & Merchandizing studio courses (must submit a portfolio in advance), Education, Engineering (very limited except civil and industrial engineering), Finanace, Music(except for Intro to Music and non-audition ensembles, such as University Singers choir), Theatre, Graduate Programs
3. Online Courses: Online courses and faculty-led travel courses have additional fees not covered in the tuition/fees exchange agreement.
4. OSU Tulsa Campus: Some courses are offered on the Tulsa campus, which is an hour away with limited transportation options.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around March for spring and around March for fall.

content.about_text: Oklahoma State University (OSU) is a public university whose main campus is located in Stillwater, Oklahoma, United States. Its Study Abroad and National Student Exchange Office coordinates reciprocal exchange for incoming students, and English is the language of instruction. OSU offers a broad undergraduate curriculum across many fields.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei exchange students can take courses across many departments using the OSU course catalog, with all teaching in English. Please note that several fields are not available to exchange students, including most Accounting beyond an introductory course, Computer Science, Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, Veterinary Medicine, and Agricultural Communication, while others such as Architecture, Studio Art, Communications, Design, Education, most Engineering, Finance, Music, Theatre, and graduate programs carry limited availability or portfolio requirements. Students should review the restricted course list carefully and note that online and faculty-led travel courses may carry additional fees, and some courses are taught on the Tulsa campus about an hour away.
content.campus_location: The main campus is in Stillwater, a college town in north-central Oklahoma often described as a friendly, student-centred community. A smaller set of courses is offered at the OSU Tulsa campus, roughly an hour away with limited transport options.

universities.toefl_subscores: Reading subscore 22, Listening subscore 22, Speaking subscore 24, Writing subscore 22
universities.available_areas: All areas except those listed below as restricted.
universities.restricted_areas:
The following Due to high demand and limited courses, students are allowed to enroll in a maximum of two courses from the School of Business.
 Northeastern schools, programs and courses are not available to exchange students. Please take these into consideration when planning your courses. SCHOOLS School of Law – This is a separate graduate-level degree program which is not available to exchange students. If you are studying undergraduate law at your home institution, you may browse the Undergraduate Course Catalog to find courses which have a law focus in many different areas of study. College of Professional Studies (CPS)
universities.admission_notes: Applicants must be full-time students at their home university. Other departmental requirements may apply. Criminal Justice, Engineering, and Business applications will be evaluated by their respective college in addition to the Global Experience Office (GEO).
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in May for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: Northeastern University is a private research university in Boston, Massachusetts, in the United States, recognized for its experiential learning model and its cooperative education programme, which began in 1909. The language of instruction is English, and exchange students register as full-time, non-degree undergraduate students.
content.why_yonsei: Exchange students may select courses from most schools and colleges at Northeastern, provided they meet the prerequisites, giving Yonsei students broad academic choice. Please note that some areas are not available to exchange students, including the School of Law and the College of Professional Studies, that cooperative education work experiences are not part of the exchange, and that enrollment in the School of Business is limited to a maximum of two courses. Its central Boston location offers strong access to the city's academic and cultural resources.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in the heart of Boston, within walking distance of the Museum of Fine Arts, the Boston Symphony, and the city's parks and cultural institutions.

universities.admission_notes:
Exchange students will select 5 preferred courses from the academic catalog and will be enrolled in at least 12 U.S.
credits. Course descriptions available here(http://catalog.northcentralcollege.edu/content.php?catoid=22&navoid=1967).
Please note that course availability may vary.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around May for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: North Central College is a private liberal arts college located in Naperville, Illinois, in the United States. It is a residential undergraduate institution offering programs across the arts, sciences, business, and professional fields, and it hosts students from more than fifty countries. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: North Central College welcomes incoming exchange students in J-1 status and offers a full English-language liberal arts curriculum, so it suits a Yonsei student looking for a broad course selection within a smaller campus community. Exchange students are expected to carry a full-time load of twelve to fifteen credit hours per semester and to maintain good academic standing. Its location in Naperville, about thirty minutes from Chicago and O'Hare International Airport, offers practical access to a major metropolitan area while keeping the day-to-day setting smaller and residential.
content.campus_location: The campus sits in downtown Naperville, Illinois, a suburb roughly thirty minutes by train or car from central Chicago, providing a compact residential setting with convenient links to the city and to O'Hare International Airport.

universities.university_name: New York University - Stern School of Business
universities.available_areas:
As an exchange student, you can take a wide array of courses. However, you cannot register for more than two finance classes or two economics courses (see note below) or any graduate level (MBA) courses, and may only take one non-Stern (i.e. non-business) class. This non-business class can be taken from the College of Arts and Science (CAS). Exceptions to the one non-Stern class rule will only be made for Economics classes. Our Economics courses are taught between Stern and the College of Arts and Science (CAS), and you may take a maximum of two Economics classes while on exchange. Your Economics classes can either be both at Stern, both at CAS or one from Stern and one from CAS. If you select two Economics classes from CAS, the remainder of your courses must be taken at Stern.

You can use the Albert Course Search to view course offerings by semester. Course descriptions are available in the Stern Undergraduate Bulletin, the Stern Course Index, and the College of Arts and Science (CAS) Bulletin.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in August for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: The Stern School of Business is the business school of New York University, a private research university located in Manhattan, New York City. Stern is one of NYU's largest schools and offers undergraduate and graduate business education across fields such as finance, economics, marketing, and management. The language of instruction is English. Its location in Lower Manhattan places it at the center of one of the world's leading financial and commercial hubs.
content.why_yonsei: Stern admits exchange students through its International Business Exchange Program (IBEX), and all instruction is in English, so no additional language requirement applies beyond the English test results requested at application. Course selection follows specific limits: you may take no more than two finance classes and no more than two economics courses, you cannot register for any graduate-level (MBA) courses, and you may take only one non-Stern (non-business) class, which must come from the College of Arts and Science. Exceptions to the one non-business class rule are made only for Economics, and Economics classes may be drawn from Stern, from CAS, or a mix of the two, up to two in total. Course offerings can be reviewed through the Albert Course Search and the Stern Undergraduate Bulletin before you arrive.
content.campus_location: Stern is located in Greenwich Village in Lower Manhattan, near Washington Square Park, within NYU's urban campus that is integrated into the surrounding city rather than set behind a single gated boundary.

universities.toefl_subscores: no less than 22 on any section (Strongly recommended: writing and speaking sections combined score of at least 47)
universities.available_areas:
NYU Schools/Faculties OPEN to Exchange Students through the NYU International Exchange Program
 
 Undergraduate Students: College of Arts and Science Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development Silver School of Social Work Tisch School of the Arts (Department of Cinema Studies and Open Arts Curriculum ONLY) Gallatin School of Individualized Study 
 
 Graduate Students: Graduate School of Arts and Science Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development Silver School of Social Work Tisch School of the Arts (Department of Cinema Studies and Open Arts Curriculum ONLY – limited coursework at the graduate level)
universities.restricted_areas: Stern School of Business, Tandon School of Engineering, Silver School of Social Work. The School of Medicine, School of Dentistry, and School of Continuing and Professional Studies do not participate in the NYU International Exchange Program. Talk with your school’s international office to determine whether the Medical or Dental Schools at your university have established an exchange relationship with their counterparts at NYU. The School of Law permits students with a baccalaureate in the study of Law to apply through the International Exchange program for one semester of study if their university has a listed partnership with NYU Law School. J.D., Ph.D. and postdoctoral students whose universities have an exchange relationship with NYU Law School may apply through the Hauser Global Law Program.
universities.admission_notes:
Undergraduate students must have successfully completed two years of academic work by the time students participate in the exchange program.
 
 Graduate students must have successfully completed the first year of a master's or doctoral program by the time students participate in the exchange program.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: New York University (NYU) is a private research university based in New York City, United States. It is organised into multiple schools and colleges and is known for its integration into the urban fabric of Manhattan rather than a single enclosed campus. English is the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: Yonsei exchange students apply to NYU through the NYU International Exchange Program after being nominated by their home university, and they receive an official NYU transcript on completion. Several schools are open to exchange students, including the College of Arts and Science, the Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development, the Silver School of Social Work, the Gallatin School of Individualized Study, and limited departments of the Tisch School of the Arts. Please note that the Stern School of Business, the Tandon School of Engineering, the School of Medicine, the School of Dentistry, and the School of Continuing and Professional Studies do not participate in this program, and other schools such as Law carry their own conditions.
content.campus_location: NYU is centred in and around Greenwich Village in Lower Manhattan, where university buildings are woven into the surrounding city. The setting places students directly within one of the largest cultural and commercial centres in the United States.

universities.available_areas:
Course descriptions can be found at: http://catalog.montana.edu/coursedescriptions/
 Scheduled courses for a specific term can be found at: https://apexprod.msu.montana.edu/apex/r/esg/s_class_schedule_bz/class-schedule
universities.restricted_areas: Spaces in the following subject areas are extremely limited for exchange students: architecture, fine arts, graphic design, theater, film, and photography. Students wishing to enroll in advanced coursework in these areas must be majors in these subjects at Yonsei and be able to present a portfolio of completed work. Students should contact the relevant department during the application process to make sure they can be accepted into suitable course work. Classes offered by the College of Nursing are not open to exchange students.
universities.admission_notes: Other acceptable proof of language proficiency: https://www.montana.edu/international/admissions/englishproficiency.html
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around January for fall.

content.about_text: Montana State University is a public land-grant research university in Bozeman, Montana, in the United States. It was established in 1893 as the Agricultural College of the State of Montana under the Morrill Act, and it now offers baccalaureate degrees in around sixty fields together with master's and doctoral programmes across its colleges. The university combines instruction, research, and public service, with recognised strengths in fields such as agriculture, engineering, computer science, and the sciences.
content.why_yonsei: Instruction at Montana State University is in English, and its course offerings are strong in areas such as agriculture, engineering, computer science, economics, physics, and political science, making it a good fit for Yonsei students in those fields. Direct exchange students from partner universities are expected to hold an overall GPA equivalent to a 3.0 on a 4.0 US scale. Please note that places in architecture, fine arts, graphic design, theatre, film, and photography are extremely limited and require relevant major status and a portfolio, and that classes offered by the College of Nursing are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: Montana State University is located in Bozeman, a college town in the Rocky Mountain region of southwestern Montana. The setting offers ready access to mountains and outdoor recreation, including nearby ski areas and national parkland.

universities.toefl_subscores: No subscore below 17
universities.available_areas: Agriculture and Natural Resources, Arts and Letters, Communication Arts and Sciences, Engineering, Natural Science, Social Science, James Madison College, Lyman Briggs College

Course availability varies greatly based on college. Information on website is currently correct regarding restrictions.
Business 
 Pre-professional programs are currently unavailable to visiting students. These include: Law, Nursing; Education; Osteopathic Medicine; Human Medicine, etc.
 Other areas of study currently excluded include Game Development and Design;Aerospace Studies; Healthcare Management; Construction Management; MBA courses.

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in August for spring and in March for fall.

content.about_text: Michigan State University is a public land-grant research university founded in 1855 in East Lansing, Michigan, and served as a model for the land-grant institutions later established under the Morrill Act of 1862. It is a large comprehensive university offering programs across agriculture, the arts, engineering, the natural sciences, and the social sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Through the American Semester Program and Exchanges, Yonsei students take courses alongside degree-seeking MSU students and are integrated into campus life, with course options across colleges such as Agriculture and Natural Resources, Arts and Letters, Communication Arts and Sciences, Engineering, Natural Science, Social Science, James Madison College, and Lyman Briggs College. Please note that several areas are not open to exchange students, including Business, Law, Nursing, Education, Osteopathic and Human Medicine, Game Development and Design, Aerospace Studies, Healthcare Management, Construction Management, and MBA courses. Course availability varies considerably by college, so students should consult the program website for current restrictions.
content.campus_location: The university occupies a large campus in East Lansing, adjacent to the Michigan state capital of Lansing, known for its expansive green spaces and the Red Cedar River running through it.

universities.available_areas: All undergraduate courses are available to exchange students. Students must meet all pre-requisite requirement and their enrollment is dependent upon seats available within the course.
universities.restricted_areas: Any course above the undergraduate level. Internship courses without prior approval.
universities.housing_info:
All exchange students will be housed in Mercer University on-campus housing.
All efforts are made to place students with an American student of the same gender. The second desire for placements is to place students with native English speakers of the same gender.

content.about_text: Mercer University is a private university in the United States founded in 1833, with its principal undergraduate campus in Macon, Georgia. It enrolls students across a range of undergraduate and graduate fields and is known for small class sizes and a personalized teaching approach. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: All undergraduate courses are open to exchange students, subject to prerequisites and seat availability, which gives Yonsei students broad access across disciplines. Please note that courses above the undergraduate level are not available to exchange students, and internship courses require prior approval. The smaller average class size and active student-organisation life offer a close-knit campus experience.
content.campus_location: The main undergraduate campus is located in Macon, a city in central Georgia in the southeastern United States. The campus offers a traditional residential American university setting.

universities.toefl_subscores: writing score 20 and above
universities.available_areas: All except advanced courses for seniors; nursing courses. Course selection is also subject to any required pre-requisites.
universities.restricted_areas: Nursing courses and some senior level courses are not available to exchange students; many courses have prerequisites.
universities.admission_notes:
1. See all language requirements at https://www.linfield.edu/ipo/elcp/test-score.html
2. See https://www.linfield.edu/ipo/elcp/test-score.html?_ga=2.226522462.237287569.1669852336-442980543.1668728607 for scores for ELCP only courses or some ELCP and some select courses. 
3. See also https://www.linfield.edu/ipo/elcp/english-language-and-culture.html.

content.about_text: Linfield University is a private liberal arts university founded in 1858 and located in McMinnville, Oregon, in the United States. It is a small institution with an undergraduate enrollment of roughly 1,600 students, and English is the language of instruction. The International Programs Office (IPO) coordinates exchange and study-abroad activity from the McMinnville campus.
content.why_yonsei: As an English-medium liberal arts university, Linfield offers Yonsei students a close-knit, small-campus experience with personal access to faculty across a broad range of subjects. Please note that nursing courses are not open to exchange students, and certain senior-level (advanced) courses are also unavailable; in addition, many courses carry prerequisites, so students should confirm eligibility for their intended courses in advance. The IPO welcomes short-term exchange students each year and provides academic and student-mentor support throughout the stay.
content.campus_location: Linfield's main campus sits on a 189-acre site in McMinnville, a town in the Willamette Valley wine country of Oregon, roughly an hour southwest of Portland.

universities.available_areas: We do not have an engineering program. Our business management and entrepreneurial studies programs are new and very popular. We have a wide range of majors and courses throughout the life and physical sciences, mathematics, computing and data analytics, all of the social sciences and humanities, media and more. Seats in studio art, dance and theatre are limited. Have an alternate choice if selecting units in these areas.
universities.restricted_areas: No FSEMs. Ineligible for "0" level education classes. Ineligible for graduate (master’s) level courses. Note: seats are limited in studio art, dance, theatre and psychology.  Students may not elect more than 2 units in these areas and should be prepared to select alternates if primary course choices are filled.

content.about_text: Hobart and William Smith Colleges (HWS) are independent liberal arts colleges located in Geneva, New York, in the Finger Lakes region of the United States. The colleges offer a highly individualized liberal arts education across the life and physical sciences, mathematics, computing and data analytics, the social sciences, humanities, media, and the arts, with a low student-to-faculty ratio. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: HWS offers a personalized liberal arts experience taught in English, with small classes and a wide range of majors across the sciences, social sciences, and humanities, including newer and popular business management and entrepreneurial studies programs. The colleges do not offer an engineering program. Seats in studio art, dance, theatre, and psychology are limited, and exchange students may not take more than two units in these areas and should prepare alternate course choices; first-year seminars (FSEMs), zero-level education classes, and graduate-level courses are not available to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The colleges occupy a 320-acre campus in Geneva, New York, on Seneca Lake in the Finger Lakes region. The setting is a small lakeside town with a close-knit campus community.

universities.available_areas:
Georgia Tech Exchange students must take least 50% of total courses in the major they select in their application. Beyond this minimum, you are free to take any courses you want at Georgia Tech that you want, as long as:
 
 Your home institution approves it, and
 You are academically prepared for that class (i.e. you have any pre-requisites).
universities.restricted_areas: Availability of courses in some majors (such as computer science) may be limited due to demand.
universities.admission_notes: *IMPORTANT NOTE* Advice for all students attempting to study one year starting from SPRING semester: Study cycle in GIT starts in the fall semester and ends in the spring semester, so even if the one year option(spring - fall) is marked as available, it is not recommended to study one year starting from spring due to the following risks: On-campus accommodation during the summer vacation may not be available, and student may need to move out during this period. students wishing to live on-campus for the summer without enrollment, they would need permission from the Dean of Students Office.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around April for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: The Georgia Institute of Technology is a public research university in Atlanta, Georgia, in the United States, established by the Georgia Legislature in 1885. It is one of the leading institutions in the United States for engineering, computing, and the sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Georgia Tech is a strong destination for a Yonsei student focused on engineering and technology, with teaching conducted in English. Exchange students must take at least 50 percent of their total courses in the major selected in their application, and beyond that minimum may take any courses for which they meet the prerequisites and have home-institution approval. Please note that availability of courses in some majors, such as computer science, may be limited due to demand.
content.campus_location: The university is located in Midtown Atlanta, Georgia, within an urban setting close to the city centre. Most classes are held on its central Atlanta campus.

useful_links.label: Introduction to Georgia Tech and Atlanta

universities.available_areas: Exchange students may enroll in any of GW’s six undergraduate schools and take undergraduate courses in any subject area noting that students may need to request professor/department approval to enroll in certain levels of courses.
universities.restricted_areas: Exchange students may not enroll in these courses: graduate level courses (labeled 4999 and above), courses in the fields of interior design & architecture, law, nursing, medicine, or courses in the Semester in Washington Program (denoted as PGMT).
universities.admission_notes: GWU would accept the TOEFL results within 12 months
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: The George Washington University is a private research university and the largest institution of higher education in Washington, D.C., in the United States. It is organised into several undergraduate and graduate schools spanning a broad range of subject areas. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: George Washington University offers a Yonsei exchange student direct enrollment across its undergraduate schools, with the ability to take undergraduate courses in many subject areas, subject to professor or department approval for certain course levels. Its location in the U.S. capital provides notable access to government, international affairs, and policy institutions. Please note that exchange students may not enroll in graduate-level courses (numbered 4999 and above) or in interior design and architecture, law, nursing, or medicine, nor in courses within the Semester in Washington Program (PGMT).
content.campus_location: The university's main Foggy Bottom campus sits in central Washington, D.C., close to the White House, the State Department, and other federal institutions. The urban campus is integrated into the surrounding city.

 FINANCIAL REQUIREMENTS - due to visa requirements, Mason must confirm students have sufficient financial resources. See website for amounts required each term
 See website for additional English test scores accepted: https://masonabroad.gmu.edu/index.cfm?FuseAction=programs.ViewProgram&Program_ID=10001
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around April for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: George Mason University is a public research university in Fairfax, Virginia, in the United States. Founded as a branch of the University of Virginia and becoming independent in 1972, it has grown into the largest four-year public university in the Commonwealth of Virginia. Instruction is in English, and the university offers a broad range of programs across the arts and sciences, business, and engineering.
content.why_yonsei: George Mason offers Yonsei exchange students access to a wide range of departments, with all instruction in English and its location near Washington, D.C. Please note that several areas are not open to incoming exchange students, including undergraduate Nursing courses, courses at the Antonin Scalia Law School, and BMGT courses in the Master of Science in Management program. The School of Business and the College of Engineering are in high demand and may be limited, so students interested in those fields should prepare alternative courses outside these areas.
content.campus_location: The main campus is in Fairfax, Virginia, a suburban setting in the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area. The location gives students convenient access to the institutions and cultural resources of the U.S. capital region.

universities.available_areas:
1. Goizueta Business School Courses Only. (Exchange students are allowed to take two non-business classes in addition to the required three business classes.)
2. Only classes at the 400-level or lower.
universities.restricted_areas: MBA, Law, or graduate-level classes
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in August for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: The Goizueta Business School is the business school of Emory University, located in Atlanta, Georgia, in the United States. It offers undergraduate (BBA), MBA, and other graduate business programs, and hosts incoming exchange students within its BBA exchange program. English is the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: Goizueta welcomes exchange students into its BBA community, where they may take business courses alongside a limited number of non-business classes, and participate in student organizations and professional development activities. Please note that exchange students take Goizueta Business School courses primarily, only at the 400 level or lower, and may take two non-business classes in addition to three required business classes; MBA, law, and graduate-level classes are not open to exchange students. Exchange students are typically hosted as J-1 exchange visitors during their time at Emory.
content.campus_location: The Goizueta Business School is located on Emory University's campus at 1300 Clifton Road in Atlanta, Georgia. The campus sits in a leafy residential area of the city.

universities.available_areas: Exchange students are only permitted to take courses in the College of Arts and Sciences(https://www.emory.edu/home/academics/degrees-programs.html)
universities.restricted_areas: Goizueta Business School courses or other courses outside of ECAS.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in August for spring and around January for fall.

content.about_text: Emory University is a private research university in Atlanta, Georgia, in the United States, founded in 1836. It is organised into several academic divisions, including the Emory College of Arts and Sciences, and the language of instruction is English. The incoming exchange program operates through existing agreements with Emory College of Arts and Sciences.
content.why_yonsei: Emory teaches in English, so a Yonsei exchange student needs to meet the university's English language requirements. Please note that exchange students are only permitted to take courses in the College of Arts and Sciences (ECAS); courses in the Goizueta Business School and other units outside ECAS are not open to exchange students. A Yonsei student should plan a course load entirely within the College of Arts and Sciences.
content.campus_location: Emory's main campus is located in the Druid Hills area of Atlanta, Georgia, a green, residential setting a short distance from the city centre. The campus serves as the base for the College of Arts and Sciences where exchange students study.

useful_links.label: Emory College of Arts and Sciences - Degrees and Programs

universities.restricted_areas:
Academic Restrictions
Students should have significant academic preparation in the discipline of their chosen major/program at Cornell. Students will take at least 50% of their courses in the department or college that is hosting them. Other courses may come from across the college or university.
Undergraduate students may apply to any undergraduate college covered by the exchange agreement. Generally, undergraduate exchange students are not approved to take graduate-level courses.
 
 Exchange students will be permitted to enroll in any course for which they are qualified, subject to meeting admission requirements, prerequisites, and timetable and capacity constraints at Cornell. However, there are specific academic restrictions to bear in mind: 
 
 Exchange students are unable to take courses in Computer Science (CS). Related subjects may be found in other departments (Electrical and Computer Engineering; Information Science, Systems, and Technology; or Operations Research and Information Engineering).
 Only students in business degree programs can apply to the Dyson School of Applied Economics and Management or the Nolan School of Hotel Administration which are both located in our College of Business.
 Studio classes (architecture, art, design, and fashion) are typically not available to students through general exchange agreements.
 Courses associated with professional graduate degree programs (MBA, MEng, MPS, MPH, etc.) are not available to non-degree students; in general, students should not expect to take graduate (MA, MS, PhD) courses. You will find this information and the additional information in our Fact Sheet.
universities.admission_notes:
Students entering Cornell for a full academic or calendar year are expected to maintain good academic standing. Good academic standing for incoming exchange and visiting students is defined as:

·         Earn a term GPA of 2.0 or higher in their first term at Cornell.

·         Successfully completing a minimum of 12 academic credits. To complete course credits, students must receive a letter grade of “D-” or higher, OR an “S” in a class taken S/U.

Failure to maintain good academic standing for your first term at Cornell will result in dismissal from the program.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: Cornell University, founded in 1865, is an Ivy League research university in Ithaca, New York, with eight undergraduate colleges, nearly 80 majors, and more than 4,000 courses.
content.why_yonsei: Cornell offers Yonsei students an Ivy League exchange in Ithaca, building an interdisciplinary plan from across its eight undergraduate colleges. Exchange students should have solid academic preparation in their chosen field, a GPA equivalent to 3.0/4.0, and strong English (TOEFL iBT 100 or IELTS 7.5). Students join through the reciprocal International Exchange Program coordinated by the Office of Global Learning.
content.campus_location: Cornell's campus crowns a hill above the town of Ithaca in the Finger Lakes region of upstate New York, framed by gorges and waterfalls, about four hours north-west of New York City.

universities.admission_notes: Preference for admission into Colgate University will be given to students enrolling in courses in the departments of Biology, Chemistry, Environmental Studies, Geography, Geology and Physics/ Astronomy. A normal course load at Colgate is four courses. View our full list of course offerings. At Colgate, UOW students must enroll in at least one course in the arts or the humanities, consistent with a liberal arts education.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around September for spring.

content.about_text: Colgate University is a highly selective private liberal arts college founded in 1819 in the village of Hamilton, in upstate New York. With around 3,000 undergraduates on a residential campus, it offers a broad liberal arts curriculum taught in small classes.
content.why_yonsei: Colgate offers Yonsei students a classic American residential liberal arts experience — small, discussion-based classes and a wide choice of subjects on a close-knit campus. The Office of International Student Services supports exchange students from the visa process through daily life. Please note that exchange places are primarily offered for the spring term.
content.campus_location: Colgate's campus sits in the small village of Hamilton in rural upstate New York, offering an intimate, community-focused setting roughly midway between Syracuse and Utica.

universities.available_areas:
Any undergraduate-level course. With nearly 100 majors, minors and concentrations across a range of
 disciplines, you can take classes in practically anything, from accounting to world literature and
 everything in between. Explore the full list of programs here: https://case.edu/programs/
universities.restricted_areas: Any graduate-level course
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in March for fall.

content.about_text: Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) is a private research university located in Cleveland, Ohio, in the United States. It offers nearly 100 majors, minors, and concentrations across a broad range of disciplines. English is the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: CWRU offers Yonsei exchange students access to a wide range of undergraduate courses taught in English, and students may take classes across many disciplines through the university's broad programme list. The Office of Education Abroad partners with international student support services to help exchange students settle into Cleveland. Please note that only undergraduate-level courses are open to exchange students, as graduate-level courses are not available.
content.campus_location: The university is located in the University Circle district of Cleveland, Ohio, a neighbourhood that concentrates cultural, medical, and educational institutions. Cleveland is a major city on the shore of Lake Erie in the north-eastern United States.

universities.available_areas: All subject areas are open to Yonsei exchange students.
universities.restricted_areas: None, but students must receive permission from relevant faculty for all courses.
universities.admission_notes: Junior status preferred.
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in July for spring and in January for fall.

content.about_text: Baruch College is a public college of the City University of New York (CUNY), located in Manhattan, New York City. Its Zicklin School of Business, established in 1919, is among the largest business schools in the United States and enrolls more than 10,000 students. The college is situated in the Flatiron and Gramercy Park area of Manhattan, and instruction is in English.
content.why_yonsei: Baruch College places Yonsei exchange students in the heart of Manhattan's financial and cultural center, within easy reach of Wall Street, Midtown, and major company headquarters. All subject areas are open to Yonsei exchange students, which makes the college flexible for a wide range of study plans. Please note that students must receive permission from the relevant faculty for all courses, so early coordination on course selection is recommended.
content.campus_location: Baruch College is situated on Lexington Avenue near the Flatiron and Gramercy Park district of Manhattan. The campus is fully urban and integrated into one of the world's most dynamic financial and cultural centers.

universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive in June for spring and in February for fall.

content.about_text: Arizona State University (ASU) is a large public research university in the United States, enrolling more than 70,000 undergraduate and graduate students from across the United States and more than 120 countries. It offers a comprehensive range of programs across numerous colleges and schools. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: ASU teaches in English and offers a very broad course catalogue, so exchange students have wide flexibility in building their study plan, with most exchange students based at or near the Tempe campus where the largest number of classes is offered. International exchange students must obtain a J-1 Exchange Visitor visa, and health insurance is mandatory and cannot be waived. Applicants should review the Incoming Exchange Program Fact Sheet for course availability and restrictions before applying.
content.campus_location: ASU's largest campus is in Tempe, Arizona, in the Phoenix metropolitan area, where most exchange students live and take courses. The university also operates additional campuses across the Phoenix area.

content.about_text: Amherst College, founded in 1821, is a highly selective private liberal arts college in the town of Amherst, Massachusetts. It is known for its open curriculum, which has no general education requirements, and for membership in the Five College Consortium alongside neighbouring institutions.
content.why_yonsei: Amherst offers Yonsei students an intimate American liberal arts experience with small classes and an open curriculum that lets students build their own course of study across disciplines. Through the Five College Consortium, students may also take courses at four neighbouring colleges and universities. Courses are chosen in keeping with the college's liberal arts focus.
content.campus_location: Amherst's campus sits in the small college town of Amherst in the Pioneer Valley of western Massachusetts, a classic New England setting about two hours from Boston.

universities.toefl_subscores: at least 20 on each subsection

Students cannot take courses in the School of Communication or at the Washington College of Law. For more information see: https://www.american.edu/abroadatau/academics.cfm
universities.restricted_areas: Communications courses
universities.transcript_info: Transcripts usually arrive around May for spring and in March for fall.

content.about_text: American University is a private research university founded in 1893 in Washington, D.C., affiliated historically with the United Methodist Church. It is organized into several schools and colleges, with recognized strengths in international relations, public affairs, business, and the social sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Located in the United States capital, American University offers Yonsei exchange students a wide selection of English-taught undergraduate courses (100-400 level) across the College of Arts and Sciences, the School of Public Affairs, the School of International Service, and the Kogod School of Business. Please note that courses in the School of Communication and at the Washington College of Law are not open to exchange students. Applicants are generally expected to be in good academic standing and to meet the university's English-language requirements.
content.campus_location: The campus sits in a residential neighborhood in the northwest part of Washington, D.C., placing students within reach of the city's government institutions, museums, and international organizations.

useful_links.label: School of International Service - International Programs

content.about_text: Universidad de Monterrey (UDEM) is a private university in the Monterrey metropolitan area of Mexico. It offers a broad range of fields including art, architecture and design, business, social sciences, health sciences, engineering, law and humanities, with its business school holding AACSB accreditation. Courses are taught in both Spanish and English, and UDEM hosts international exchange students from a wide partner network.
content.why_yonsei: UDEM is a good option for Yonsei students interested in studying in a Spanish-speaking environment while still having access to some courses taught in English. Students who take courses in Spanish are generally expected to have at least a B1 level in Spanish, and Spanish-language support is available. Available fields include business, engineering, health sciences, law and social sciences, education and humanities; please note that courses in Art, Architecture and Design are limited and very restricted.
content.campus_location: UDEM is located in San Pedro Garza Garcia, a prosperous municipality within the Monterrey metropolitan area in the state of Nuevo Leon, northern Mexico. The campus is set within a major industrial and business region of the country.

content.about_text: CETYS Universidad (Centro de Ensenanza Tecnica y Superior) is a private university founded in 1961 in the Mexican state of Baja California. It operates a three-campus system in Mexicali, Tijuana, and Ensenada, with academic strengths in business, engineering, law, psychology, and the social sciences. Most instruction is in Spanish, with a selection of courses available in English.
content.why_yonsei: CETYS offers Yonsei exchange students the chance to study close to the United States border in northern Mexico, with English-taught options concentrated in the International Business and Industrial Engineering programs at the Mexicali campus and selected courses at the Ensenada campus. Course offerings differ by campus and by area, so students should confirm availability directly with the relevant campus international office. Students with Spanish proficiency may access a broader range of courses in business, engineering, law, and psychology.
content.campus_location: The three campuses are spread across Baja California in the cities of Mexicali, Tijuana, and Ensenada, with two of them located along the United States-Mexico border.

content.about_text: The University of Waterloo is a public research university in Waterloo, Ontario, Canada, founded in 1957. It is organized into six faculties and is widely known for its programmes in mathematics, engineering, and computer science, as well as its large co-operative education system. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Waterloo offers Yonsei students English-taught coursework with particular strength in mathematics, computer science, and engineering. Exchange students should consult Waterloo's undergraduate course-selection and restricted-courses pages, because certain courses are limited or closed to incoming exchange students and some require prerequisites or department approval.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in the city of Waterloo, Ontario, in the Kitchener-Waterloo region of southern Canada, a recognized centre for technology and research.

content.about_text: The Université de Montréal (University of Montreal) is a public research university located in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. It is a large, comprehensive French-language institution offering programmes across a wide range of disciplines. The primary language of instruction is French.
content.why_yonsei: As a French-language university, Université de Montréal requires exchange students to have French proficiency equivalent to at least level B2 on the CEFR scale, and some programmes require a higher level or official language tests. Exchange students may take French language courses offered by the university's language school, with tuition-free 3-credit French courses available. Yonsei students considering this exchange should be prepared to study primarily in French and should review the programmes of study that carry particular requirements.
content.campus_location: The main campus is situated on the slopes of Mount Royal in Montreal, the largest city in the province of Quebec. Montreal is a bilingual, French-speaking metropolis in eastern Canada.

content.about_text: The I.H. Asper School of Business is the business faculty of the University of Manitoba in Winnipeg, Canada. It is accredited by AACSB International, a standard achieved by a small share of business schools worldwide. All exchange courses at Asper are taught in English.
content.why_yonsei: Asper is a strong choice for Yonsei students focusing on business, with most Asper courses available to exchange students who meet prerequisites and where space is available; some courses may require professor approval. Students should take business courses. Courses outside Asper, such as Economics, Psychology, or History, may be possible but are subject to those departments' approval and may be hard to access. Please note that exam formats vary, final exams are held in the last two weeks of term, and requests for early departure or exam rewrites are not permitted, so students must remain in Winnipeg until all exams are complete.
content.campus_location: The Asper School of Business is located at the University of Manitoba's Fort Garry campus in Winnipeg, Manitoba.

 2. Unavailable Faculties and schools: Health Sciences, Education, I.H. Asper School of Business

content.about_text: The University of Manitoba is a public research university in Winnipeg, Canada. Founded in 1877, it was the first university established in western Canada and is the largest university in the province of Manitoba. It is a broad-based institution offering programmes across many faculties, with English as the language of instruction.
content.why_yonsei: Manitoba offers Yonsei exchange students English-taught courses across faculties including Agricultural and Food Sciences, Art, Arts (Humanities and Social Sciences), Environment, Earth and Resources, Kinesiology and Recreation Management, Law, Music, and Science. Please note that Architecture, Engineering, and Social Work are limited; that Health Sciences, Education, and the I.H. Asper School of Business are not available through this agreement; and that Honours courses in Psychology and English, as well as Computer Science courses (1000-4000 level), cannot be taken. The International Centre supports inbound exchange students with advising and orientation.
content.campus_location: The main Fort Garry campus is located on the southern edge of Winnipeg, the capital of Manitoba, along the Red River.

content.about_text: The University of Calgary is a public research university founded in 1966 and a member of the U15 group of research-intensive Canadian universities. Located in Calgary, Alberta, it enrolls more than 30,000 students across 14 faculties and offers over 250 academic programs, with particular strengths in engineering, business, health sciences, and energy-related fields. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Instruction is in English, and exchange students can choose courses across a broad range of faculties including Arts, Science, Kinesiology, and others. Please note that several programs are closed to exchange students, including Medicine, Nursing, and Veterinary Medicine, along with a number of graduate programs, and courses in the Haskayne School of Business, Kinesiology, and the Schulich School of Engineering are limited and carry additional restrictions. Exchange students typically take three to five courses per semester, with four recommended.
content.campus_location: The main campus is located in the northwest of Calgary, a major city in Alberta near the Canadian Rocky Mountains. The setting offers a modern urban campus with ready access to outdoor recreation in the surrounding region.

The Alberta School of Business also requires that you: i) have completed introductory-level statistics, calculus, microeconomics, and macroeconomics by the time of the application (everything else said about the Alberta School of Business is still correct). Everything said about the Faculty of Law is still correct but they have provided an additional warning which reads, "Please note that students interested in an undergraduate-level exchange to UAlberta's Faculty of Law Juris Doctor program should know that it is an after-degree program. The other students in the program will each have at least a 3-year bachelor's degree. Students without similar academic backgrounds are strongly encouraged to discuss their plans with academic advisors at their home institutions before applying for the exchange and seek permision to, if need be, take fewer than the maximum number of courses allowable per term while on exchange."
The additional and/or higher requirements for our Faculty of Law and Faculty of Kinesiology, Sport, and Recreation( https://www.ualberta.ca/en/admissions-programs/exchange-programs/incoming-exchange-application-guide/admission-requirements/admission-requirements.html)
universities.notes: 2.9/4.3, Business: 3.2/4.3, Engineering: 3.4/4.3

content.about_text: The University of Alberta is one of Canada's top research universities, based in Edmonton, the capital of the province of Alberta. Its large North Campus sits on the banks of the North Saskatchewan River.
content.why_yonsei: Alberta offers Yonsei students a fully English-taught exchange in Edmonton, taking three to five courses per term across many faculties. Please note that courses in the Schools of Business, Engineering, Law, and Kinesiology are only available if you are admitted to that faculty for your exchange, and that Art & Design, Computing Science, Drama, and Economics courses are in very high demand and difficult to secure.
content.campus_location: The university's North Campus spreads across some 150 buildings on the south bank of the North Saskatchewan River in Edmonton, one of the northernmost major cities in the world and a gateway to the Canadian Rockies.

content.about_text: The University of British Columbia (UBC) is one of Canada's leading research universities, with campuses in Vancouver and the Okanagan. It consistently ranks among the world's top universities.
content.why_yonsei: UBC offers Yonsei students a fully English-taught exchange in spectacular Vancouver (or at the Okanagan campus), earning credit across undergraduate or graduate courses open to exchange students. A minimum GPA of around B (3.0/4.0) and English proficiency are required, and exchange students pay no tuition, though health insurance and transit fees apply. Please note that the Faculty of Arts has some course-registration restrictions for exchange students.
content.campus_location: UBC's main Vancouver campus sits on a forested headland at Point Grey, surrounded by ocean and mountains at the edge of one of the world's most scenic cities.

content.about_text: The Beedie School of Business is the business school of Simon Fraser University, a public research university in British Columbia, Canada. Beedie serves several thousand undergraduate and graduate students and offers courses across concentrations including entrepreneurship and innovation, finance, human resources management, international business, management information systems, marketing, operations management, and strategic analysis. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Beedie suits a Yonsei student focused on business who wants an English-taught semester in Canada, with a broad spread of business concentrations to choose from and a structured mentorship program for incoming exchange students. Please note that for business exchange and study abroad students, at least seventy-five per cent of your courses must be BUS (business) courses; you may take additional courses from other faculties at SFU, subject to standard restrictions. The school's Office of International Programs and its Beedie Abroad Network provide pre-arrival guidance and peer mentors to support your transition.
content.campus_location: The Beedie School of Business is based at Simon Fraser University's main campus on Burnaby Mountain in Burnaby, British Columbia, in the Greater Vancouver area, with additional SFU campuses located in downtown Vancouver and Surrey.

content.about_text: Simon Fraser University (SFU) is a public research university in British Columbia, Canada, with its main campus in Burnaby and additional campuses in Vancouver and Surrey. It offers a wide range of undergraduate programs across the arts, social sciences, sciences, business, communication, and applied sciences. The language of instruction is English.
content.why_yonsei: Courses are taught in English, and undergraduate exchange students may take courses numbered 100 to 400 across multiple subject areas, subject to meeting any course prerequisites with equivalent coursework documented on their home transcript. Some subjects and courses carry restrictions for inbound exchange and study abroad students; these are updated each summer and should be checked before course selection. Students should review the academic information and course outlines pages provided by SFU when planning their study.
content.campus_location: The main Burnaby campus sits atop Burnaby Mountain, a short walk from on-campus residence and roughly 45 to 60 minutes by public transport from the Vancouver and Surrey campuses. The setting combines a self-contained mountaintop campus with access to the greater Vancouver area.

content.about_text: McGill University, founded in 1821, is one of Canada's leading research universities, based in Montreal, Quebec. This exchange is hosted by its Desautels Faculty of Management, McGill's business school.
content.why_yonsei: McGill's Desautels Faculty of Management offers Yonsei students a business-focused exchange in the bilingual, cosmopolitan city of Montreal, taught in English. Exchange students take the majority of their courses within Desautels, with a maximum of one or two courses per term in other faculties (Arts, Science) where open. A full-time load is 15 McGill credits (about 30 ECTS).
content.campus_location: The Desautels Faculty of Management is based in the Bronfman Building on Sherbrooke Street in downtown Montreal, at the foot of Mount Royal in the heart of the city.

content.about_text: McGill University is a public research university founded in 1821 in Montreal, Quebec, Canada, making it one of the country's oldest institutions of higher education. It is a comprehensive university known for research across a broad range of disciplines. The primary language of instruction is English, although students may submit assignments and examinations in either English or French.
content.why_yonsei: McGill offers Yonsei exchange students an English-taught environment within a bilingual city, with courses available across a wide range of faculties. Exchange students are generally required to take the majority of their credits within the faculty to which they are admitted. Please note that this exchange covers faculties other than Management; exchange in the Desautels Faculty of Management is not included under this agreement, so students should consult the fact sheet and McGill Abroad website for the most current course and faculty restrictions.
content.campus_location: The downtown campus is located at the foot of Mount Royal in central Montreal, set within walking distance of the city center.

content.about_text: Huron University College, founded in 1863, is a small liberal arts university affiliated with Western University in London, Ontario, Canada. It teaches in the arts, social sciences, and business with a focus on small classes.
content.why_yonsei: Huron offers Yonsei students a small-college, English-taught exchange in Canada, choosing courses in the arts, social sciences, and business, while also benefiting from its affiliation with the larger Western University. Peer Guides help exchange students settle into the close-knit Huron community.
content.campus_location: Huron sits on the campus of Western University in London, Ontario, a mid-sized, student-friendly Canadian city roughly midway between Toronto and Detroit.

content.about_text: Brock University is a public research university located in St. Catharines, Ontario, Canada, in the Niagara region. It offers programmes across six faculties, including business administration, social sciences, humanities, sciences, and education, with English as the language of instruction. The university is also known for distinctive offerings such as oenology and viticulture.
content.why_yonsei: Brock University offers Yonsei exchange students an English-medium curriculum with a broad range of courses across its faculties, including business administration, computer science, international relations, and sport management. Incoming students apply after being nominated by their home institution and can choose courses from the university's published timetables, supported by Brock International. Please note that internship and experiential placement courses (such as Co-op and community placements), GAME program courses, and Teacher Service Education courses are not open to exchange students.
content.campus_location: The main campus sits atop the Niagara Escarpment in St. Catharines, Ontario, within the Niagara wine region and close to Niagara Falls. The setting combines an established campus with access to surrounding natural and recreational areas.
